   140	
   141	function hasTable(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
   142	  const row = database.prepare(
   143	    "SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name = ?"
   144	  ).get(tableName) as { present?: number } | undefined;
   145	
   146	  return row?.present === 1;
   147	}
   148	
   149	function hasIndex(database: Database.Database, indexName: string): boolean {
   150	  const row = database.prepare(
   151	    "SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name = ?"
   152	  ).get(indexName) as { present?: number } | undefined;
   153	
   154	  return row?.present === 1;
   155	}
   156	
   157	function getTableColumns(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string[] {
   158	  return (database.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>)
   159	    .map((column) => column.name)
   160	    .filter((columnName) => typeof columnName === 'string' && columnName.length > 0);
   161	}
   162	
   163	function getTableSql(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string | null {
   164	  const row = database.prepare(
   165	    "SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name = ?"
   166	  ).get(tableName) as { sql?: string } | undefined;
   167	
   168	  return typeof row?.sql === 'string' ? row.sql : null;
   169	}
   170	
   171	function hasEmbeddingCacheDimensionsPrimaryKey(database: Database.Database): boolean {
   172	  const tableSql = getTableSql(database, 'embedding_cache');
   173	  if (typeof tableSql !== 'string') {
   174	    return false;
   175	  }
   176	
   177	  return /PRIMARY\s+KEY\s*\(\s*content_hash\s*,\s*model_id\s*,\s*dimensions\s*\)/i.test(tableSql);
   178	}
   179	
   180	function ensureEmbeddingCacheSchema(database: Database.Database): void {
   181	  if (!hasTable(database, 'embedding_cache')) {
   182	    initEmbeddingCache(database);
   183	    return;
   184	  }
   185	
   186	  if (hasEmbeddingCacheDimensionsPrimaryKey(database)) {
   187	    return;
   188	  }
   189	
   190	  logger.info('Migrating embedding_cache primary key to include dimensions');
   191	  database.exec(`
   192	    ALTER TABLE embedding_cache RENAME TO embedding_cache_legacy_dimensions;
   193	  `);
   194	  initEmbeddingCache(database);
   195	  database.exec(`
   196	    INSERT OR REPLACE INTO embedding_cache (
   197	      content_hash,
   198	      model_id,
   199	      embedding,
   200	      dimensions,
   201	      created_at,
   202	      last_used_at
   203	    )
   204	    SELECT
   205	      content_hash,
   206	      model_id,
   207	      embedding,
   208	      dimensions,
   209	      created_at,
   210	      last_used_at
   211	    FROM embedding_cache_legacy_dimensions
   212	  `);
   213	  database.exec('DROP TABLE embedding_cache_legacy_dimensions');
   214	}
   215	
   216	function hasConstitutionalTierConstraint(database: Database.Database): boolean {
   217	  const tableSql = getTableSql(database, 'memory_index');
   218	  return typeof tableSql === 'string' && tableSql.includes("'constitutional'");
   219	}
   220	
   221	function createRequiredIndex(
   222	  database: Database.Database,
   223	  indexName: string,
   224	  sql: string,
   225	  context: string,
   226	): void {
   227	  try {
   228	    database.exec(sql);
   229	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   230	    if (hasIndex(database, indexName)) {
   231	      logger.warn(`${context}: reusing existing index after DDL warning`, {
   232	        indexName,
   233	        error: get_error_message(error),
   234	      });
   235	      return;
   236	    }
   237	    throw error;
   238	  }
   239	
   240	  if (!hasIndex(database, indexName)) {
   241	    throw new Error(`${context}: expected index "${indexName}" to exist after migration`);
   242	  }
   243	}
   244	
   245	function createMemoryConflictsTable(database: Database.Database): void {
   246	  database.exec(`
   247	    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_conflicts (
   248	      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   249	      timestamp TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
   250	      action TEXT CHECK(action IN ('CREATE', 'CREATE_LINKED', 'UPDATE', 'SUPERSEDE', 'REINFORCE')),
   251	      new_memory_hash TEXT,
   252	      new_memory_id INTEGER,
   253	      existing_memory_id INTEGER,
   254	      similarity REAL,
   255	      reason TEXT,
   256	      new_content_preview TEXT,
   257	      existing_content_preview TEXT,
   258	      contradiction_detected INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   259	      contradiction_type TEXT,
   260	      spec_folder TEXT,

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/sqlite-fts.ts | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Sqlite Fts
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Semantic and lexical search (memory_search)
     5	// Weighted BM25 scoring for FTS5 full-text search.
     6	// Extracted from hybrid-search.ts ftsSearch() for independent
     7	// Testing and future delegation.
     8	
     9	import { BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS, normalizeLexicalQueryTokens } from './bm25-index.js';
    10	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    11	
    12	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    13	// 2. INTERFACES
    14	
    15	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16	interface FtsBm25Result {
    17	  id: number;
    18	  fts_score: number;
    19	  [key: string]: unknown;
    20	}
    21	
    22	interface FtsBm25Options {
    23	  limit?: number;
    24	  specFolder?: string;
    25	  includeArchived?: boolean;
    26	}
    27	
    28	type LexicalPath = 'fts5' | 'like' | 'unavailable';
    29	type FallbackState =
    30	  | 'ok'
    31	  | 'compile_probe_miss'
    32	  | 'missing_table'
    33	  | 'no_such_module_fts5'
    34	  | 'bm25_runtime_failure';
    35	
    36	interface LexicalCapabilitySnapshot {
    37	  lexicalPath: LexicalPath;
    38	  fallbackState: FallbackState;
    39	}
    40	
    41	let lastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot: LexicalCapabilitySnapshot | null = null;
    42	
    43	function cloneLexicalCapabilitySnapshot(
    44	  snapshot: LexicalCapabilitySnapshot | null
    45	): LexicalCapabilitySnapshot | null {
    46	  return snapshot ? { ...snapshot } : null;
    47	}
    48	
    49	function setLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot(snapshot: LexicalCapabilitySnapshot): void {
    50	  lastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot = { ...snapshot };
    51	}
    52	
    53	function getLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot(): LexicalCapabilitySnapshot | null {
    54	  return cloneLexicalCapabilitySnapshot(lastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot);
    55	}
    56	
    57	function resetLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot(): void {
    58	  lastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot = null;
    59	}
    60	
    61	function toErrorMessage(error: unknown): string {
    62	  return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    63	}
    64	
    65	function readCompileOptionValue(row: unknown): string | null {
    66	  if (typeof row === 'string') {
    67	    return row;
    68	  }
    69	  if (!row || typeof row !== 'object') {
    70	    return null;
    71	  }
    72	
    73	  const record = row as Record<string, unknown>;
    74	  const direct = record.compile_options;
    75	  if (typeof direct === 'string') {
    76	    return direct;
    77	  }
    78	
    79	  const firstString = Object.values(record).find((value) => typeof value === 'string');
    80	  return typeof firstString === 'string' ? firstString : null;
    81	}
    82	
    83	function isNoSuchModuleFts5Error(error: unknown): boolean {
    84	  return toErrorMessage(error).toLowerCase().includes('no such module: fts5');
    85	}
    86	
    87	function isBm25RuntimeFailure(error: unknown): boolean {
    88	  const message = toErrorMessage(error).toLowerCase();
    89	  return message.includes('bm25');
    90	}
    91	
    92	function probeFts5Capability(db: Database.Database): LexicalCapabilitySnapshot {
    93	  try {
    94	    const compileRows = (db.prepare('PRAGMA compile_options') as Database.Statement).all() as unknown[];
    95	    const hasFts5CompileFlag = compileRows
    96	      .map(readCompileOptionValue)
    97	      .some((value) => typeof value === 'string' && value.toUpperCase().includes('ENABLE_FTS5'));
    98	
    99	    if (!hasFts5CompileFlag) {
   100	      return {
   101	        lexicalPath: 'unavailable',
   102	        fallbackState: 'compile_probe_miss',
   103	      };
   104	    }
   105	  } catch {
   106	    return {
   107	      lexicalPath: 'unavailable',
   108	      fallbackState: 'compile_probe_miss',
   109	    };
   110	  }
   111	
   112	  try {
   113	    const result = (db.prepare(
   114	      `SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'`
   115	    ) as Database.Statement).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
   116	
   117	    if (!result) {
   118	      return {
   119	        lexicalPath: 'unavailable',
   120	        fallbackState: 'missing_table',
   121	      };
   122	    }
   123	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   124	    if (isNoSuchModuleFts5Error(error)) {
   125	      return {
   126	        lexicalPath: 'unavailable',
   127	        fallbackState: 'no_such_module_fts5',
   128	      };
   129	    }
   130	    return {
   131	      lexicalPath: 'unavailable',
   132	      fallbackState: 'missing_table',
   133	    };
   134	  }
   135	
   136	  return {
   137	    lexicalPath: 'fts5',
   138	    fallbackState: 'ok',
   139	  };
   140	}
   141	
   142	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   143	// 3. CORE FUNCTION
   144	
   145	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   146	/**
   147	 * Execute a weighted BM25 FTS5 search against memory_fts.
   148	 *
   149	 * Uses SQLite FTS5's built-in bm25() ranking function with
   150	 * per-column weights instead of the default `rank` pseudo-column.
   151	 * This gives title matches 10x weight, trigger_phrases 5x, etc.
   152	 *
   153	 * @param db - SQLite database connection
   154	 * @param query - Raw search query (will be sanitized)
   155	 * @param options - Search options (limit, specFolder, includeArchived)
   156	 * @returns Array of results with BM25 scores (higher = better)
   157	 * @example
   158	 * ```ts
   159	 * const rows = fts5Bm25Search(db, 'memory search', { limit: 10 });
   160	 * ```
   161	 */
   162	function fts5Bm25Search(
   163	  db: Database.Database,
   164	  query: string,
   165	  options: FtsBm25Options = {}
   166	): FtsBm25Result[] {
   167	  const { limit = 20, specFolder, includeArchived = false } = options;
   168	
   169	  // Sanitize via shared tokenizer, then wrap each token in quotes and join with OR for recall
   170	  const tokens = normalizeLexicalQueryTokens(query).fts;
   171	  const sanitized = tokens
   172	    .map(t => (t.startsWith('"') && t.endsWith('"')) ? t : `"${t}"`)
   173	    .join(' OR ');
   174	
   175	  if (!sanitized) {
   176	    setLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot(probeFts5Capability(db));
   177	    return [];
   178	  }
   179	
   180	  const folderFilter = specFolder
   181	    ? 'AND (m.spec_folder = ? OR m.spec_folder LIKE ? || "/%")'
   182	    : '';
   183	  const archivalFilter = !includeArchived
   184	    ? 'AND (m.is_archived IS NULL OR m.is_archived = 0)'
   185	    : '';
   186	  const deprecatedTierFilter =
   187	    "AND (m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier != 'deprecated')";
   188	
   189	  const params: (string | number)[] = specFolder
   190	    ? [sanitized, specFolder, specFolder, limit]
   191	    : [sanitized, limit];
   192	
   193	  // Bm25() returns negative scores (lower = better), so we negate
   194	  // To produce positive scores where higher = better match.
   195	  const [w0, w1, w2, w3] = BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS;
   196	  const sql = `
   197	    SELECT m.*, -bm25(memory_fts, ${w0}, ${w1}, ${w2}, ${w3}) AS fts_score
   198	    FROM memory_fts
   199	    JOIN memory_index m ON m.id = memory_fts.rowid
   200	    WHERE memory_fts MATCH ?
   201	      ${folderFilter}
   202	      ${archivalFilter}
   203	      ${deprecatedTierFilter}
   204	    ORDER BY fts_score DESC
   205	    LIMIT ?
   206	  `;
   207	
   208	  const capability = probeFts5Capability(db);
   209	  setLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot(capability);
   210	  if (capability.fallbackState !== 'ok') {
   211	    console.warn(`[sqlite-fts] FTS5 unavailable (${capability.fallbackState}); returning empty lexical lane results`);
   212	    return [];
   213	  }
   214	
   215	  try {
   216	    const rows = (db.prepare(sql) as Database.Statement).all(
   217	      ...params
   218	    ) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
   219	
   220	    const normalizedRows = rows.map(row => ({
   221	      ...row,
   222	      id: row.id as number,
   223	      fts_score: (row.fts_score as number) || 0,
   224	    }));
   225	    setLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot({
   226	      lexicalPath: 'fts5',
   227	      fallbackState: 'ok',
   228	    });
   229	    return normalizedRows;
   230	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   231	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   232	    const failureSnapshot: LexicalCapabilitySnapshot = isNoSuchModuleFts5Error(error)
   233	      ? {
   234	          lexicalPath: 'unavailable',
   235	          fallbackState: 'no_such_module_fts5',
   236	        }
   237	      : isBm25RuntimeFailure(error)
   238	        ? {
   239	            lexicalPath: 'unavailable',

   245	              fallbackState: 'missing_table',
   246	            }

   248	              lexicalPath: 'unavailable',
   249	              fallbackState: 'bm25_runtime_failure',
   250	            };
   251	    setLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot(failureSnapshot);
   252	    console.warn(`[sqlite-fts] BM25 FTS5 search failed: ${msg}`);
   253	    return [];
   254	  }

vec_memories|CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_memories USING vec0(
        embedding FLOAT[1024]
      )
vec_memories_chunks|CREATE TABLE "vec_memories_chunks"(chunk_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,size INTEGER NOT NULL,validity BLOB NOT NULL,rowids BLOB NOT NULL)
vec_memories_vector_chunks00|CREATE TABLE "vec_memories_vector_chunks00"(rowid PRIMARY KEY,vectors BLOB NOT NULL)
memory_lineage|CREATE TABLE memory_lineage (
      memory_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      logical_key TEXT NOT NULL,
      version_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
      root_memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      predecessor_memory_id INTEGER,
      superseded_by_memory_id INTEGER,
      valid_from TEXT NOT NULL,
      valid_to TEXT,
      transition_event TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(transition_event IN ('CREATE', 'UPDATE', 'SUPERSEDE', 'BACKFILL')),
      actor TEXT DEFAULT 'system',
      metadata TEXT,
      created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
      UNIQUE(logical_key, version_number)
    )
active_memory_projection|CREATE TABLE active_memory_projection (
      logical_key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      root_memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      active_memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    )
embedding_cache|CREATE TABLE embedding_cache (
      content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
      model_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      embedding BLOB NOT NULL,
      dimensions INTEGER NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
      last_used_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
      PRIMARY KEY (content_hash, model_id, dimensions)
    )
causal_edges|CREATE TABLE causal_edges (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            source_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            target_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            relation TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(relation IN (
              'caused', 'enabled', 'supersedes', 'contradicts', 'derived_from', 'supports'
            )),
            strength REAL DEFAULT 1.0 CHECK(strength >= 0.0 AND strength <= 1.0),
            evidence TEXT,
            extracted_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
            created_by TEXT DEFAULT 'manual',
            last_accessed TEXT, valid_at TEXT DEFAULT NULL, invalid_at TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
            UNIQUE(source_id, target_id, relation)
          )
memory_fts|CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5(
            title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text,
            content='memory_index', content_rowid='id'
          )
memory_fts_insert|CREATE TRIGGER memory_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON memory_index BEGIN
            INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid, title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
            VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.trigger_phrases, new.file_path, new.content_text);
          END
memory_fts_update|CREATE TRIGGER memory_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON memory_index BEGIN
            INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts, rowid, title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
            VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.trigger_phrases, old.file_path, old.content_text);
            INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid, title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
            VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.trigger_phrases, new.file_path, new.content_text);
          END
memory_fts_delete|CREATE TRIGGER memory_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON memory_index BEGIN
            INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts, rowid, title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
            VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.trigger_phrases, old.file_path, old.content_text);
          END
memory_summaries|CREATE TABLE memory_summaries (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,
            summary_text TEXT NOT NULL,
            summary_embedding BLOB,
            key_sentences TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
            created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
            updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
          )
working_memory|CREATE TABLE working_memory (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    memory_id INTEGER,
    attention_score REAL DEFAULT 1.0,
    added_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    last_focused TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    focus_count INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    event_counter INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    mention_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    source_tool TEXT,
    source_call_id TEXT,
    extraction_rule_id TEXT,
    redaction_applied INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    UNIQUE(session_id, memory_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (memory_id) REFERENCES memory_index(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
  )

 succeeded in 0ms:
CREATE TABLE memory_index (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      spec_folder TEXT NOT NULL,
      file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
      canonical_file_path TEXT,
      anchor_id TEXT,
      title TEXT,
      trigger_phrases TEXT,
      importance_weight REAL DEFAULT 0.5,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      embedding_model TEXT,
      embedding_generated_at TEXT,
      embedding_status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK(embedding_status IN ('pending', 'success', 'failed', 'retry', 'partial')),
      retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      last_retry_at TEXT,
      failure_reason TEXT,
      base_importance REAL DEFAULT 0.5,
      decay_half_life_days REAL DEFAULT 90.0,
      is_pinned INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      access_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      last_accessed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      importance_tier TEXT DEFAULT 'normal' CHECK(importance_tier IN ('constitutional', 'critical', 'important', 'normal', 'temporary', 'deprecated')),
      tenant_id TEXT,
      user_id TEXT,
      agent_id TEXT,
      session_id TEXT,
      shared_space_id TEXT,
      context_type TEXT DEFAULT 'general' CHECK(context_type IN ('research', 'implementation', 'planning', 'general')),
      channel TEXT DEFAULT 'default',
      content_hash TEXT,
      provenance_source TEXT,
      provenance_actor TEXT,
      governed_at TEXT,
      retention_policy TEXT DEFAULT 'keep',
      delete_after TEXT,
      governance_metadata TEXT,
      expires_at DATETIME,
      confidence REAL DEFAULT 0.5,
      validation_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      -- FSRS (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler) columns for cognitive memory
      stability REAL DEFAULT 1.0,
      difficulty REAL DEFAULT 5.0,
      last_review TEXT,
      review_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      file_mtime_ms INTEGER,
      is_archived INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      document_type TEXT DEFAULT 'memory',
      spec_level INTEGER,
      content_text TEXT,
      quality_score REAL DEFAULT 0,
      quality_flags TEXT,
      parent_id INTEGER REFERENCES memory_index(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      chunk_index INTEGER,
      chunk_label TEXT,
      encoding_intent TEXT DEFAULT 'document',
      learned_triggers TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
      interference_score REAL DEFAULT 0, related_memories TEXT, memory_type TEXT DEFAULT 'declarative'
            CHECK(memory_type IN (
              'working', 'episodic', 'prospective', 'implicit', 'declarative',
              'procedural', 'semantic', 'autobiographical', 'meta-cognitive'
            )), half_life_days REAL, type_inference_source TEXT DEFAULT 'default'
            CHECK(type_inference_source IN (
              'frontmatter_explicit', 'importance_tier', 'file_path', 'keywords', 'default', 'manual'
            )),
      UNIQUE(spec_folder, file_path, anchor_id)
    )

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Hybrid Search
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Combines vector, FTS, and BM25 search with fallback
     5	
     6	// 1. IMPORTS
     7	
     8	// Local
     9	import { getIndex, isBm25Enabled } from './bm25-index.js';
    10	import { fuseResultsMulti } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/rrf-fusion';
    11	import { getAdaptiveWeights, isAdaptiveFusionEnabled } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/adaptive-fusion';
    12	import { CO_ACTIVATION_CONFIG, spreadActivation } from '../cognitive/co-activation.js';
    13	import { applyMMR } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/mmr-reranker';
    14	import { INTENT_LAMBDA_MAP, classifyIntent } from './intent-classifier.js';
    15	import { fts5Bm25Search } from './sqlite-fts.js';
    16	import { DEGREE_CHANNEL_WEIGHT } from './graph-search-fn.js';
    17	import {
    18	  isMMREnabled,
    19	  isCrossEncoderEnabled,
    20	  isLocalRerankerEnabled,
    21	  isSearchFallbackEnabled,
    22	  isDocscoreAggregationEnabled,
    23	  isDegreeBoostEnabled,
    24	  isContextHeadersEnabled,
    25	} from './search-flags.js';
    26	import { rerankLocal } from './local-reranker.js';
    27	import { computeDegreeScores } from './graph-search-fn.js';
    28	import type { GraphSearchFn } from './search-types.js';
    29	
    30	// Feature catalog: Hybrid search pipeline
    31	
    32	export type { GraphSearchFn } from './search-types.js';
    33	
    34	import { routeQuery } from './query-router.js';
    35	import { isComplexityRouterEnabled } from './query-classifier.js';
    36	import { enforceChannelRepresentation } from './channel-enforcement.js';
    37	import {
    38	  truncateByConfidence,
    39	  isConfidenceTruncationEnabled,
    40	  DEFAULT_MIN_RESULTS,
    41	  GAP_THRESHOLD_MULTIPLIER,
    42	} from './confidence-truncation.js';
    43	import {
    44	  getDynamicTokenBudget,
    45	  isDynamicTokenBudgetEnabled,
    46	  DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET_CONFIG,
    47	} from './dynamic-token-budget.js';
    48	import { ensureDescriptionCache, getSpecsBasePaths } from './folder-discovery.js';
    49	import {
    50	  isFolderScoringEnabled,
    51	  lookupFolders,
    52	  computeFolderRelevanceScores,
    53	  enrichResultsWithFolderScores,
    54	  twoPhaseRetrieval,
    55	} from './folder-relevance.js';
    56	
    57	import { collapseAndReassembleChunkResults } from '../scoring/mpab-aggregation.js';
    58	
    59	// Type-only
    60	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    61	import type { SpreadResult } from '../cognitive/co-activation.js';
    62	import type { MMRCandidate } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/mmr-reranker';
    63	import type { FusionResult } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/rrf-fusion';
    64	import type { ChannelName } from './query-router.js';
    65	import type { EnforcementResult } from './channel-enforcement.js';
    66	import type { TruncationResult } from './confidence-truncation.js';
    67	
    68	// 2. INTERFACES
    69	
    70	type VectorSearchFn = (
    71	  embedding: Float32Array | number[],
    72	  options: Record<string, unknown>
    73	) => Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
    74	
    75	interface HybridSearchOptions {
    76	  limit?: number;
    77	  specFolder?: string;
    78	  minSimilarity?: number;
    79	  useBm25?: boolean;
    80	  useFts?: boolean;
    81	  useVector?: boolean;
    82	  useGraph?: boolean;
    83	  includeArchived?: boolean;
    84	  includeContent?: boolean;
    85	  /**
    86	   * Evaluation-only mode.
    87	   * When true, preserve the requested top-K window by bypassing confidence
    88	   * truncation and token-budget truncation without changing live defaults.
    89	   */
    90	  evaluationMode?: boolean;
    91	  /** Classified query intent for adaptive fusion weight selection (e.g. 'understand', 'fix_bug'). */
    92	  intent?: string;
    93	  /** Optional trigger phrases for query-classifier trigger-match routing path. */
    94	  triggerPhrases?: string[];
    95	  /**
    96	   * Internal fallback override: when true, bypass complexity routing and
    97	   * enable all retrieval channels for this search call.
    98	   */
    99	  forceAllChannels?: boolean;
   100	  /**
   101	   * Internal raw-candidate mode used by the Stage 1 pipeline.
   102	   * When true, stop after channel collection and return pre-fusion candidates.
   103	   */
   104	  skipFusion?: boolean;
   105	  /**
   106	   * Internal pipeline handoff mode.
   107	   * When true, return immediately after adaptive/RRF fusion so Stage 2/3 can
   108	   * apply the remaining pipeline scoring and aggregation steps.
   109	   */
   110	  stopAfterFusion?: boolean;
   111	}
   112	
   113	interface HybridSearchResult {
   114	  id: number | string;
   115	  /**
   116	   * Normalized relevance score (0-1). Semantics depend on `source`:
   117	   * - `'vector'` — cosine similarity from sqlite-vec (normalized from 0-100 to 0-1)
   118	   * - `'bm25'` — BM25 term-frequency relevance (min-max normalized per source group)
   119	   * - `'fts'` — FTS5 rank score (absolute value, min-max normalized per source group)
   120	   * - `'graph'` — graph traversal relevance
   121	   *
   122	   * After hybrid merge, all source scores are min-max normalized to 0-1 within
   123	   * their source group to ensure fair cross-method comparison (see P3-02 fix).
   124	   */
   125	  score: number;
   126	  source: string;
   127	  title?: string;
   128	  [key: string]: unknown;
   129	}
   130	
   131	/** Non-enumerable shadow metadata attached to result arrays via Object.defineProperty. */
   132	interface ShadowMetaArray {
   133	  _s4shadow?: unknown;
   134	  _s4attribution?: unknown;
   135	  _degradation?: unknown;
   136	}
   137	
   138	/** Normalize a fused RRF result to the HybridSearchResult contract. */
   139	function toHybridResult(result: FusionResult): HybridSearchResult {
   140	  const sourceCandidate = (result as { source?: unknown }).source;
   141	  const primarySource = result.sources[0] ?? 'hybrid';
   142	  const scoreCandidate = (result as { score?: unknown }).score;
   143	
   144	  return {
   145	    ...result,
   146	    id: result.id,
   147	    score: typeof scoreCandidate === 'number' ? scoreCandidate : result.rrfScore,
   148	    source: typeof sourceCandidate === 'string' ? sourceCandidate : primarySource,
   149	  };
   150	}
   151	
   152	// 3. SPRINT 3 PIPELINE METADATA
   153	
   154	/**
   155	 * Optional metadata about pipeline stages attached to enhanced search results.
   156	 * Only populated when the corresponding feature flags are enabled.
   157	 */
   158	interface Sprint3PipelineMeta {
   159	  /** Query complexity routing result (SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER). */
   160	  routing?: {
   161	    tier: string;
   162	    channels: string[];
   163	    skippedChannels: string[];
   164	    featureFlagEnabled: boolean;
   165	    confidence: string;
   166	    features: Record<string, unknown>;
   167	  };
   168	  /** Channel enforcement result (SPECKIT_CHANNEL_MIN_REP). */
   169	  enforcement?: { applied: boolean; promotedCount: number; underRepresentedChannels: string[] };
   170	  /** Confidence truncation result (SPECKIT_CONFIDENCE_TRUNCATION). */
   171	  truncation?: {
   172	    truncated: boolean;
   173	    originalCount: number;
   174	    truncatedCount: number;
   175	    medianGap: number;
   176	    cutoffGap: number;
   177	    cutoffIndex: number;
   178	    thresholdMultiplier: number;
   179	    minResultsGuaranteed: number;
   180	    featureFlagEnabled: boolean;
   181	  };
   182	  /** Dynamic token budget result (SPECKIT_DYNAMIC_TOKEN_BUDGET). */
   183	  tokenBudget?: {
   184	    tier: string;
   185	    budget: number;
   186	    applied: boolean;
   187	    featureFlagEnabled: boolean;
   188	    configValues: Record<string, number>;
   189	    headerOverhead: number;
   190	    adjustedBudget: number;
   191	  };
   192	}
   193	
   194	// 4. PI-A2: DEGRADATION TYPES
   195	
   196	/** Fallback tier in the 3-tier degradation chain. */
   197	type FallbackTier = 1 | 2 | 3;
   198	
   199	/** Why degradation was triggered at a given tier. */
   200	interface DegradationTrigger {
   201	  reason: 'low_quality' | 'insufficient_results' | 'both';
   202	  topScore: number;
   203	  resultCount: number;
   204	  relativeGap?: number;
   205	}
   206	
   207	/** Record of a single degradation event during tiered fallback. */
   208	interface DegradationEvent {
   209	  tier: FallbackTier;
   210	  trigger: DegradationTrigger;
   211	  resultCountBefore: number;
   212	  resultCountAfter: number;
   213	}
   214	
   215	/**
   216	 * Absolute quality floor for degradation checks.
   217	 *
   218	 * Raw RRF scores are typically small decimals (often <0.05), so a
   219	 * high fixed threshold causes false degradations. Use a conservative floor and
   220	 * pair it with a relative-gap check to avoid score-scale coupling.
   221	 */
   222	const DEGRADATION_QUALITY_THRESHOLD = 0.02;
   223	
   224	/** Minimum relative separation between top-1 and top-2 scores. */
   225	const DEGRADATION_MIN_RELATIVE_GAP = 0.2;
   226	
   227	/** Minimum result count: must have >= this many results to stay at current tier. */
   228	const DEGRADATION_MIN_RESULTS = 3;
   229	
   230	// 5. MODULE STATE
   231	
   232	/** Default result limit when none is specified by the caller. */
   233	const DEFAULT_LIMIT = 20;
   234	/** Primary vector similarity floor for hybrid fallback passes (percentage units). */
   235	const PRIMARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY = 30;
   236	/** Secondary vector similarity floor for adaptive retry passes (percentage units). */
   237	const SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY = 17;
   238	/** Tier-2 vector similarity floor for quality-aware fallback (percentage units). */
   239	const TIERED_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY = 10;
   240	
   241	/** Minimum MMR candidates required for diversity reranking to be worthwhile. */
   242	const MMR_MIN_CANDIDATES = 2;
   243	
   244	/** Fallback lambda (diversity vs relevance) when intent is not in INTENT_LAMBDA_MAP. */
   245	const MMR_DEFAULT_LAMBDA = 0.7;
   246	
   247	/** Number of top results used as seeds for co-activation spreading. */
   248	const SPREAD_ACTIVATION_TOP_N = 5;
   249	
   250	/** Maximum contextual tree header length prepended to content (including brackets). */
   251	const CONTEXT_HEADER_MAX_CHARS = 100;
   252	/** Header/content separator characters added during contextual tree injection. */
   253	const CONTEXT_HEADER_SEPARATOR_CHARS = 1;
   254	/** Reserved token overhead per contextual header, calibrated to max header length. */
   255	const CONTEXT_HEADER_TOKEN_OVERHEAD = Math.ceil(
   256	  (CONTEXT_HEADER_MAX_CHARS + CONTEXT_HEADER_SEPARATOR_CHARS) / 4
   257	);
   258	
   259	let db: Database.Database | null = null;
   260	let vectorSearchFn: VectorSearchFn | null = null;

   266	
   267	function createMemoryConflictIndexes(
   268	  database: Database.Database,
   269	  context: string,
   270	): void {
   271	  createRequiredIndex(
   272	    database,
   273	    'idx_conflicts_memory',
   274	    'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_conflicts_memory ON memory_conflicts(existing_memory_id)',
   275	    context,
   276	  );
   277	  createRequiredIndex(
   278	    database,
   279	    'idx_conflicts_timestamp',
   280	    'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_conflicts_timestamp ON memory_conflicts(timestamp DESC)',
   281	    context,
   282	  );
   283	}
   284	
   285	function getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(
   286	  availableColumns: Set<string>,
   287	  candidates: string[],
   288	  fallback: string,
   289	): string {
   290	  for (const candidate of candidates) {
   291	    if (availableColumns.has(candidate)) {
   292	      return candidate;
   293	    }
   294	  }
   295	  return fallback;
   296	}
   297	
   298	function hasUnifiedMemoryConflictsTable(database: Database.Database): boolean {
   299	  if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_conflicts')) {
   300	    return false;
   301	  }
   302	
   303	  const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_conflicts'));
   304	  return REQUIRED_MEMORY_CONFLICT_COLUMNS.every((columnName) => columns.has(columnName));
   305	}
   306	
   307	function migrateMemoryConflictsTable(database: Database.Database): void {
   308	  if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_conflicts')) {
   309	    createMemoryConflictsTable(database);
   310	    return;
   311	  }
   312	
   313	  if (hasUnifiedMemoryConflictsTable(database)) {
   314	    return;
   315	  }
   316	
   317	  database.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_conflicts RENAME TO memory_conflicts_legacy');
   318	  createMemoryConflictsTable(database);
   319	
   320	  const legacyColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_conflicts_legacy'));
   321	  database.exec(`
   322	    INSERT INTO memory_conflicts (
   323	      id,
   324	      timestamp,
   325	      action,
   326	      new_memory_hash,
   327	      new_memory_id,
   328	      existing_memory_id,
   329	      similarity,
   330	      reason,
   331	      new_content_preview,
   332	      existing_content_preview,
   333	      contradiction_detected,
   334	      contradiction_type,
   335	      spec_folder,
   336	      created_at
   337	    )
   338	    SELECT
   339	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['id'], 'NULL')},
   340	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['timestamp', 'created_at'], 'CURRENT_TIMESTAMP')},
   341	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['action'], 'NULL')},
   342	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_memory_hash'], 'NULL')},
   343	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_memory_id'], 'NULL')},
   344	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['existing_memory_id'], 'NULL')},
   345	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['similarity', 'similarity_score'], 'NULL')},
   346	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['reason', 'notes'], 'NULL')},
   347	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_content_preview'], 'NULL')},
   348	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['existing_content_preview'], 'NULL')},
   349	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['contradiction_detected'], '0')},
   350	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['contradiction_type'], 'NULL')},
   351	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['spec_folder'], 'NULL')},
   352	      ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['created_at', 'timestamp'], 'CURRENT_TIMESTAMP')}
   353	    FROM memory_conflicts_legacy
   354	  `);
   355	  database.exec('DROP TABLE memory_conflicts_legacy');
   356	
   357	  if (!hasUnifiedMemoryConflictsTable(database)) {
   358	    throw new Error('Migration v12: memory_conflicts table is still missing unified columns after rebuild');
   359	  }
   360	}
   361	
   362	function summarizeCompatibilityFailures(report: SchemaCompatibilityReport): string {
   363	  const details: string[] = [];
   364	
   365	  if (report.missingTables.length > 0) {
   366	    details.push(`missing tables: ${report.missingTables.join(', ')}`);
   367	  }
   368	  if (Object.keys(report.missingColumns).length > 0) {
   369	    const missingColumns = Object.entries(report.missingColumns)
   370	      .map(([tableName, columns]) => `${tableName}[${columns.join(', ')}]`)
   371	      .join('; ');
   372	    details.push(`missing columns: ${missingColumns}`);
   373	  }
   374	  if (report.missingIndexes.length > 0) {
   375	    details.push(`missing indexes: ${report.missingIndexes.join(', ')}`);
   376	  }
   377	  if (report.constraintMismatches.length > 0) {
   378	    details.push(`constraint mismatches: ${report.constraintMismatches.join(', ')}`);
   379	  }
   380	
   381	  return details.join(' | ');
   382	}
   383	
   384	function logDuplicateColumnMigrationSkip(columnName: string, error: unknown): void {
   385	  logger.warn(`Migration skipped existing ${columnName} column`, {
   386	    error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
   387	  });
   388	}
   389	
   390	function getMigrationAllowedBasePaths(): string[] {
   391	  const workspaceRoot = process.cwd();
   392	  const envPaths = process.env.MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS
   393	    ? process.env.MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS.split(path.delimiter)
   394	    : [];
   395	
   396	  return Array.from(new Set([
   397	    ...getSpecsBasePaths(workspaceRoot),
   398	    path.join(workspaceRoot, '.opencode'),
   399	    path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude'),
   400	    workspaceRoot,
   401	    ...envPaths,
   402	  ].filter(Boolean).map((basePath) => path.resolve(basePath))));
   403	}
   404	
   405	// Schema version for migration tracking
   406	// Added memory_type column for type-specific half-lives (REQ-002)
   407	// Added file_mtime_ms for incremental indexing fast-path (REQ-023, T064-T066)
   408	// Added 'partial' embedding_status for deferred indexing (REQ-031, T096)
   409	// Added causal_edges table for Causal Memory Graph (REQ-012, T043-T047)
   410	// Added memory_corrections table for learning from corrections (REQ-015, REQ-026, T052-T055)
   411	// V10: Schema consolidation and index optimization
   412	// V11: Error code deduplication and validation improvements
   413	// V12: Unified memory_conflicts DDL (KL-1 Schema Unification)
   414	// V13: Add document_type and spec_level columns for full spec folder document indexing
   415	// V14: Add content_text column + FTS5 rebuild for BM25 full-text search across restarts
   416	// V15: Add quality_score and quality_flags columns for memory quality gates
   417	// V16: Add parent_id column for chunked indexing of large files (010-index-large-files)
   418	// V17: Add interference_score column for TM-01 (the rollout)
   419	// V18: the rollout — weight_history table + causal_edges provenance + encoding_intent column
   420	// V19: degree_snapshots + community_assignments (N2 graph centrality)
   421	// V20: memory_summaries + memory_entities + entity_catalog (R8/R10/S5)
   422	// V21: Add learned_triggers column (R11 learned feedback)
   423	// V22: Step 2 memory lineage tables + active projection support
   424	// V23: One-time spec_folder re-canonicalization + session_state migration
   425	// V24: Add trigger-cache source and temporal contiguity indexes
   426	/** Current schema version for vector-index migrations. */
   427	export const SCHEMA_VERSION = 25;
   428	
   429	// Run schema migrations from one version to another
   430	// Each migration is idempotent - safe to run multiple times
   431	// BUG-019 FIX: Wrap migrations in transaction for atomicity
   432	/**
   433	 * Runs schema migrations between two schema versions.
   434	 * @param database - The database connection to migrate.
   435	 * @param from_version - The current schema version.
   436	 * @param to_version - The target schema version.
   437	 * @returns Nothing.
   438	 */
   439	export function run_migrations(database: Database.Database, from_version: number, to_version: number): void {
   440	  const migrations: Record<number, () => void> = {
   441	    1: () => {
   442	      // V0 -> v1: Initial schema (already exists via create_schema)
   443	    },
   444	    2: () => {
   445	      // V1 -> v2: Add idx_history_timestamp index
   446	      try {
   447	        database.exec('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_history_timestamp ON memory_history(timestamp DESC)');
   448	        logger.info('Migration v2: Created idx_history_timestamp index');
   449	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   450	        if (!get_error_message(e).includes('already exists')) {
   451	          console.warn('[VectorIndex] Migration v2 warning:', get_error_message(e));
   452	        }
   453	      }
   454	    },
   455	    3: () => {
   456	      // V2 -> v3: Add related_memories column
   457	      try {
   458	        database.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN related_memories TEXT');
   459	        logger.info('Migration v3: Added related_memories column');
   460	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   461	        if (!get_error_message(e).includes('duplicate column')) {
   462	          console.warn('[VectorIndex] Migration v3 warning:', get_error_message(e));
   463	        }
   464	      }
   465	    },
   466	    4: () => {
   467	      // V3 -> v4: Add FSRS (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler) columns for cognitive memory
   468	      // These columns enable spaced repetition-based memory retrieval prioritization
   469	      const fsrs_columns = [
   470	        { name: 'stability', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN stability REAL DEFAULT 1.0' },
   471	        { name: 'difficulty', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN difficulty REAL DEFAULT 5.0' },
   472	        { name: 'last_review', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN last_review TEXT' },
   473	        { name: 'review_count', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN review_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0' }
   474	      ];
   475	
   476	      for (const col of fsrs_columns) {
   477	        try {
   478	          database.exec(col.sql);
   479	          logger.info(`Migration v4: Added ${col.name} column (FSRS)`);
   480	        } catch (e: unknown) {
   481	          if (!get_error_message(e).includes('duplicate column')) {
   482	            console.warn(`[VectorIndex] Migration v4 warning (${col.name}):`, get_error_message(e));
   483	          }
   484	        }
   485	      }
   486	
   487	      // Use the canonical helper so later schema refinements only update one DDL definition.
   488	      createMemoryConflictsTable(database);
   489	      logger.info('Migration v4: Ensured memory_conflicts table');
   490	
   491	      // Create indexes for FSRS columns
   492	      createRequiredIndex(
   493	        database,
   494	        'idx_stability',
   495	        'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stability ON memory_index(stability DESC)',
   496	        'Migration v4',
   497	      );
   498	      createRequiredIndex(
   499	        database,
   500	        'idx_last_review',
   501	        'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_last_review ON memory_index(last_review)',
   502	        'Migration v4',
   503	      );
   504	      createRequiredIndex(
   505	        database,
   506	        'idx_fsrs_retrieval',
   507	        'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_fsrs_retrieval ON memory_index(stability, difficulty, last_review)',
   508	        'Migration v4',
   509	      );
   510	      logger.info('Migration v4: Created FSRS indexes');
   511	    },
   512	    5: () => {
   513	      // V4 -> v5: Add memory_type column for type-specific half-lives (REQ-002, T006)
   514	      try {
   515	        database.exec(`
   516	          ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN memory_type TEXT DEFAULT 'declarative'
   517	            CHECK(memory_type IN (
   518	              'working', 'episodic', 'prospective', 'implicit', 'declarative',
   519	              'procedural', 'semantic', 'autobiographical', 'meta-cognitive'
   520	            ))
   521	        `);
   522	        logger.info('Migration v5: Added memory_type column');
   523	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   524	        if (!get_error_message(e).includes('duplicate column')) {
   525	          console.warn('[VectorIndex] Migration v5 warning (memory_type):', get_error_message(e));
   526	        }
   527	      }
   528	
   529	      try {
   530	        database.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN half_life_days REAL');
   531	        logger.info('Migration v5: Added half_life_days column');
   532	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   533	        if (!get_error_message(e).includes('duplicate column')) {
   534	          console.warn('[VectorIndex] Migration v5 warning (half_life_days):', get_error_message(e));
   535	        }
   536	      }
   537	
   538	      try {
   539	        database.exec(`
   540	          ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN type_inference_source TEXT DEFAULT 'default'
   541	            CHECK(type_inference_source IN (
   542	              'frontmatter_explicit', 'importance_tier', 'file_path', 'keywords', 'default', 'manual'
   543	            ))
   544	        `);
   545	        logger.info('Migration v5: Added type_inference_source column');
   546	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   547	        if (!get_error_message(e).includes('duplicate column')) {
   548	          console.warn('[VectorIndex] Migration v5 warning (type_inference_source):', get_error_message(e));
   549	        }
   550	      }
   551	
   552	      try {
   553	        database.exec('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_memory_type ON memory_index(memory_type)');
   554	        database.exec('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_memory_type_decay ON memory_index(memory_type, half_life_days)');
   555	        logger.info('Migration v5: Created memory_type indexes');
   556	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   557	        console.warn('[VectorIndex] Migration v5 warning (indexes):', get_error_message(e));
   558	      }
   559	
   560	      logger.info('Migration v5: Type inference backfill will run on next index scan');
   561	    },
   562	    6: () => {
   563	      // V5 -> v6: Add file_mtime_ms for incremental indexing (REQ-023, T064-T066)
   564	      try {
   565	        database.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN file_mtime_ms INTEGER');
   566	        logger.info('Migration v6: Added file_mtime_ms column for incremental indexing');
   567	      } catch (e: unknown) {

   449	
   450	function applySessionDedup(results: MemorySearchRow[], sessionId: string, enableDedup: boolean): DedupResult {
   451	  if (!enableDedup || !sessionId || !sessionManager.isEnabled()) {
   452	    return {
   453	      results,
   454	      dedupStats: { enabled: false, sessionId: null }
   455	    };
   456	  }
   457	
   458	  const { filtered, dedupStats } = sessionManager.filterSearchResults(sessionId, results as Parameters<typeof sessionManager.filterSearchResults>[1]);
   459	
   460	  if (filtered.length > 0) {
   461	    sessionManager.markResultsSent(sessionId, filtered as Parameters<typeof sessionManager.markResultsSent>[1]);
   462	  }
   463	
   464	  return {
   465	    results: filtered as MemorySearchRow[],
   466	    dedupStats: {
   467	      ...dedupStats,
   468	      sessionId
   469	    }
   470	  };
   471	}

   482	/** Handle memory_search tool — performs hybrid vector/BM25 search with intent-aware ranking.

   486	async function handleMemorySearch(args: SearchArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {

   546	  if (!hasCursor && !hasQuery && !hasConcepts) {

   550	  if (hasCursor) {

   604	    }
   605	  }
   606	
   607	  const hasValidQuery = normalizedQuery !== null;
   608	  const hasValidConcepts = Array.isArray(concepts) && concepts.length >= 2;
   609	  const effectiveQuery = normalizedQuery ?? (hasValidConcepts ? concepts.join(', ') : '');

   611	  if (!hasValidQuery && !hasValidConcepts) {

   658	
   659	  if (explicitIntent) {

   663	      intentWeights = intentClassifier.getIntentWeights(explicitIntent);
   664	    } else {

   679	    }
   680	  }
   681	
   682	  // FIX RC3-B: Intent confidence floor — override low-confidence auto-detections to "understand"
   683	  const INTENT_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR = parseFloat(process.env.SPECKIT_INTENT_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR || '0.25');
   684	  if (detectedIntent && intentConfidence < INTENT_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR && !explicitIntent) {
   685	    console.error(`[memory-search] Intent confidence ${intentConfidence.toFixed(3)} below floor ${INTENT_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR}, overriding '${detectedIntent}' → 'understand'`);

   687	    intentConfidence = 1.0;
   688	    intentWeights = intentClassifier.getIntentWeights('understand' as IntentType);
   689	  }
   690	
   691	  // Phase C: Intent-to-profile auto-routing.
   692	  // Explicit caller `profile` always takes precedence; auto-detect fills in when absent.

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Db Helpers
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     5	
     6	// Feature catalog: Memory indexing (memory_save)
     7	// Feature catalog: Reconsolidation-on-save
     8	
     9	export {
    10	  ALLOWED_POST_INSERT_COLUMNS,
    11	  applyPostInsertMetadata,
    12	} from '../../lib/storage/post-insert-metadata.js';
    13	
    14	/**
    15	 * TM-06 safety gate: verify a pre-reconsolidation checkpoint exists.
    16	 * Accepts either exact name `pre-reconsolidation` or prefixed variants.
    17	 */
    18	export function hasReconsolidationCheckpoint(database: Database.Database, specFolder: string): boolean {
    19	  try {
    20	    const tableExists = database.prepare(
    21	      "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='checkpoints'"
    22	    ).get();
    23	
    24	    if (!tableExists) {
    25	      return false;
    26	    }
    27	
    28	    const row = database.prepare(`
    29	      SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
    30	      FROM checkpoints
    31	      WHERE (name = 'pre-reconsolidation' OR name LIKE 'pre-reconsolidation-%')
    32	        AND (spec_folder = ? OR spec_folder IS NULL OR spec_folder = '')
    33	    `).get(specFolder) as { count?: number } | undefined;
    34	
    35	    return (row?.count ?? 0) > 0;
    36	  } catch (error: unknown) {
    37	    console.warn('[db-helpers] hasReconsolidationCheckpoint lookup failed:', error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
    38	    return false;
    39	  }
    40	}

  1040	
  1041	/* --- 8. INDEX MEMORY FILE --- */
  1042	
  1043	/** Parse, validate, and index a memory file with PE gating, FSRS scheduling, and causal links */
  1044	async function indexMemoryFile(
  1045	  filePath: string,
  1046	  {
  1047	    force = false,
  1048	    parsedOverride = null as ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile> | null,
  1049	    asyncEmbedding = false,
  1050	    scope = {} as MemoryScopeMatch,
  1051	    qualityGateMode = 'enforce' as 'enforce' | 'warn-only',
  1052	  } = {},
  1053	): Promise<IndexResult> {
  1054	  // Reuse parsed content when provided by caller to avoid a second parse.
  1055	  const parsed = parsedOverride || memoryParser.parseMemoryFile(filePath);
  1056	  const database = requireDb();
  1057	  const prepared = prepareParsedMemoryForIndexing(parsed, database);
  1058	  const validation = prepared.validation;
  1059	  if (!validation.valid) {
  1060	    throw new Error(`Validation failed: ${validation.errors.join(', ')}`);
  1061	  }
  1062	  if (prepared.qualityLoopResult.fixes.length > 0 && prepared.qualityLoopResult.passed && prepared.qualityLoopResult.fixedContent) {
  1063	    console.error(`[memory-save] Quality loop applied ${prepared.qualityLoopResult.fixes.length} auto-fix(es) for ${path.basename(filePath)}`);
  1064	  }
  1065	
  1066	  return processPreparedMemory(prepared, filePath, {
  1067	    force,
  1068	    asyncEmbedding,
  1069	    persistQualityLoopContent: true,
  1070	    refreshFromDiskAfterLock: parsedOverride !== null,
  1071	    scope,
  1072	    qualityGateMode,
  1073	  });
  1074	}

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts | sed -n '320,560p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public

   328	
   329	async function finalizeMemoryFileContent(
   330	  filePath: string,
   331	  content: string,
   332	): Promise<void> {
   333	  const backupPath = `${filePath}.${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}.bak`;
   334	  const tempPath = `${filePath}.${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}.tmp`;
   335	  let backupCreated = false;
   336	  let tempCreated = false;
   337	
   338	  try {
   339	    try {
   340	      await fs.promises.copyFile(filePath, backupPath);
   341	      backupCreated = true;
   342	    } catch (backupErr: unknown) {
   343	      const errCode = typeof backupErr === 'object' && backupErr !== null && 'code' in backupErr
   344	        ? String((backupErr as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code)
   345	        : '';
   346	      if (errCode !== 'ENOENT') {
   347	        throw backupErr;
   348	      }
   349	    }
   350	
   351	    await fs.promises.writeFile(tempPath, content, 'utf-8');
   352	    tempCreated = true;
   353	    await fs.promises.rename(tempPath, filePath);
   354	    tempCreated = false;
   355	  } catch (writeErr: unknown) {
   356	    if (tempCreated) {
   357	      try {
   358	        await fs.promises.unlink(tempPath);
   359	      } catch {
   360	        // Best-effort cleanup only.
   361	      }
   362	    }
   363	    if (backupCreated) {
   364	      try {
   365	        await fs.promises.copyFile(backupPath, filePath);
   366	      } catch (restoreErr: unknown) {
   367	        console.warn(
   368	          '[memory-save] Auto-fix file restore failed after finalize error:',
   369	          restoreErr instanceof Error ? restoreErr.message : String(restoreErr),
   370	        );
   371	      }
   372	    }
   373	    throw writeErr;
   374	  } finally {
   375	    if (tempCreated) {
   376	      try {
   377	        await fs.promises.unlink(tempPath);
   378	      } catch {
   379	        // Best-effort cleanup only.
   380	      }
   381	    }
   382	    if (backupCreated) {
   383	      try {
   384	        await fs.promises.unlink(backupPath);
   385	      } catch {
   386	        // Best-effort cleanup only.
   387	      }
   388	    }
   389	  }
   390	}
   391	
   392	function appendResultWarning<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(result: T, warning: string | null): T {
   393	  if (!warning) {
   394	    return result;
   395	  }
   396	
   397	  const r = result as Record<string, unknown>;
   398	  const warnings = Array.isArray(r.warnings)
   399	    ? [...(r.warnings as string[])]
   400	    : [];
   401	  warnings.push(warning);
   402	  r.warnings = warnings;
   403	  return result;
   404	}
   405	
   406	function recordCrossPathPeSupersedes(
   407	  database: ReturnType<typeof requireDb>,
   408	  memoryId: number,
   409	  supersededMemoryId: number | null,
   410	  samePathMemoryId: number | null,
   411	  reason: string | null | undefined,
   412	): void {
   413	  if (supersededMemoryId == null || supersededMemoryId === samePathMemoryId) {
   414	    return;
   415	  }
   416	
   417	  causalEdges.init(database);
   418	  const evidence = reason && reason.trim().length > 0
   419	    ? reason.trim()

   425	    1.0,
   426	    evidence,
   427	    true,
   428	    'auto',
   429	  );
   430	}
   431	
   432	interface ChunkedInsertTracker {
   433	  parentId: number | null;
   434	  childIds: Set<number>;
   435	}
   436	
   437	function createChunkedInsertTracker(): ChunkedInsertTracker {
   438	  return {
   439	    parentId: null,
   440	    childIds: new Set<number>(),
   441	  };
   442	}
   443	
   444	function trackChunkedInsert(
   445	  tracker: ChunkedInsertTracker,
   446	  memoryId: number,
   447	  fields: Record<string, unknown>,
   448	): void {
   449	  if (typeof fields.chunk_index === 'number') {
   450	    tracker.childIds.add(memoryId);
   451	    return;
   452	  }
   453	
   454	  if (tracker.parentId == null) {
   455	    tracker.parentId = memoryId;
   456	  }
   457	}
   458	
   459	function rollbackCreatedChunkTree(
   460	  database: ReturnType<typeof requireDb>,
   461	  tracker: ChunkedInsertTracker,
   462	): { attempted: boolean; cleaned: boolean; error?: string } {
   463	  if (tracker.parentId == null) {
   464	    return { attempted: false, cleaned: false };
   465	  }
   466	
   467	  const childIds = new Set<number>(tracker.childIds);
   468	  try {
   469	    const persistedChildRows = database.prepare(`
   470	      SELECT id
   471	      FROM memory_index
   472	      WHERE parent_id = ?
   473	    `).all(tracker.parentId) as Array<{ id: number }>;
   474	    for (const row of persistedChildRows) {
   475	      childIds.add(row.id);
   476	    }
   477	  } catch {
   478	    // Best-effort lookup only. Tracked IDs remain authoritative for cleanup.
   479	  }
   480	
   481	  const cleanupErrors: string[] = [];
   482	  for (const childId of childIds) {
   483	    try {
   484	      if (!delete_memory_from_database(database, childId)) {
   485	        cleanupErrors.push(`Chunk ${childId} was not deleted`);
   486	      }
   487	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   488	      cleanupErrors.push(`Chunk ${childId} cleanup failed: ${getAtomicSaveErrorMessage(error)}`);
   489	    }
   490	  }
   491	
   492	  try {
   493	    if (!delete_memory_from_database(database, tracker.parentId)) {
   494	      cleanupErrors.push(`Parent ${tracker.parentId} was not deleted`);
   495	    }
   496	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   497	    cleanupErrors.push(`Parent ${tracker.parentId} cleanup failed: ${getAtomicSaveErrorMessage(error)}`);
   498	  }
   499	
   500	  if (cleanupErrors.length > 0) {
   501	    return {
   502	      attempted: true,
   503	      cleaned: false,
   504	      error: cleanupErrors.join('; '),
   505	    };
   506	  }
   507	
   508	  return { attempted: true, cleaned: true };
   509	}
   510	
   511	function captureAtomicSaveOriginalState(filePath: string): { existed: boolean; content: string | null } {
   512	  if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
   513	    return { existed: false, content: null };
   514	  }
   515	
   516	  return {
   517	    existed: true,
   518	    content: fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8'),
   519	  };
   520	}

   524	}

   530	  }
   531	
   532	  try {
   533	    fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
   534	    return { deleted: true, existed: true };
   535	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   536	    return {
   537	      deleted: false,
   538	      existed: true,
   539	      error: getAtomicSaveErrorMessage(error),
   540	    };
   541	  }
   542	}

  1513	/**

   183	function normalizeTimestamp(value?: string | null): string {

   199	function normalizeScopeValue(value: unknown): string | null {

   207	function buildScopePrefix(row: MemoryIndexRow): string | null {
   208	  const scopeTuple = [

   210	    ['user', normalizeScopeValue(row.user_id)],
   211	    ['agent', normalizeScopeValue(row.agent_id)],
   212	    ['session', normalizeScopeValue(row.session_id)],
   213	    ['shared_space', normalizeScopeValue(row.shared_space_id)],
   214	  ].filter((entry): entry is [string, string] => entry[1] != null);

   216	  if (scopeTuple.length === 0) {
   217	    return null;
   218	  }
   219	
   220	  const scopeHash = createHash('sha256')
   221	    .update(JSON.stringify(scopeTuple), 'utf8')
   222	    .digest('hex')
   223	    .slice(0, 24);
   224	
   225	  return `scope-sha256:${scopeHash}`;
   226	}
   227

   229	  return components.some((component) => component.includes('::'));

   243	    anchorId: parts.anchorId,
   244	  });

   251	function buildLogicalKey(row: MemoryIndexRow): string {

  1379	  const shadowMeta = initialResults as HybridSearchResult[] & ShadowMetaArray;
  1380	  const s4shadowMeta = shadowMeta._s4shadow;
  1381	  const s4attributionMeta = shadowMeta._s4attribution;
  1382	  const degradationMeta = shadowMeta._degradation;
  1383	
  1384	  // C138/T316: MMR reranking with request-scoped embedding cache.
  1385	  // Reuse embeddings already returned by the vector channel when present and
  1386	  // only query vec_memories for missing IDs.
  1387	  let reranked: HybridSearchResult[] = fusedHybridResults.slice(0, limit);
  1388	
  1389	  // P1-5: Optional local GGUF reranking path (RERANKER_LOCAL=true).
  1390	  // Preserve cross-encoder gate semantics: when SPECKIT_CROSS_ENCODER=false, skip reranking.
  1391	  if (isCrossEncoderEnabled() && isLocalRerankerEnabled() && reranked.length >= MMR_MIN_CANDIDATES) {
  1392	    const localReranked = await rerankLocal(query, reranked, limit);
  1393	    if (localReranked !== reranked) {
  1394	      reranked = localReranked as HybridSearchResult[];
  1395	    }
  1396	  }
  1397	
  1398	  if (db && isMMREnabled()) {
  1399	    const numericIds = reranked
  1400	      .map(r => r.id)
  1401	      .filter((id): id is number => typeof id === 'number');
  1402	
  1403	    if (numericIds.length >= MMR_MIN_CANDIDATES) {
  1404	      try {
  1405	        const embeddingMap = new Map<number, Float32Array>(vectorEmbeddingCache);
  1406	        const missingIds = numericIds.filter((id) => !embeddingMap.has(id));
  1407	
  1408	        if (missingIds.length > 0) {
  1409	          const placeholders = missingIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
  1410	          const embRows = (db.prepare(
  1411	            `SELECT rowid, embedding FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid IN (${placeholders})`
  1412	          ) as Database.Statement).all(...missingIds) as Array<{ rowid: number; embedding: Buffer }>;
  1413	
  1414	          for (const row of embRows) {
  1415	            if (Buffer.isBuffer(row.embedding)) {
  1416	              embeddingMap.set(
  1417	                row.rowid,
  1418	                new Float32Array(row.embedding.buffer, row.embedding.byteOffset, row.embedding.byteLength / 4)
  1419	              );
  1420	            }
  1421	          }
  1422	        }
  1423	
  1424	        const mmrCandidates: MMRCandidate[] = [];
  1425	        for (const r of reranked) {
  1426	          const emb = embeddingMap.get(r.id as number);
  1427	          if (emb) {
  1428	            mmrCandidates.push({
  1429	              id: r.id as number,
  1430	              score: (r.score as number) ?? 0,
  1431	              embedding: emb,
  1432	            });
  1433	          }
  1434	        }
  1435	
  1436	        if (mmrCandidates.length >= MMR_MIN_CANDIDATES) {
  1437	          const mmrLambda = INTENT_LAMBDA_MAP[intent] ?? MMR_DEFAULT_LAMBDA;
  1438	          const diversified = applyMMR(mmrCandidates, { lambda: mmrLambda, limit });
  1439	
  1440	          // FIX #6: Same fix as stage3-rerank FIX #5 — MMR can only diversify
  1441	          // rows that have embeddings. Non-embedded rows (lexical-only hits,
  1442	          // graph injections) must be preserved and merged back in their
  1443	          // original relative order instead of being silently dropped.
  1444	          const embeddedIdSet = new Set(mmrCandidates.map(c => c.id));
  1445	          const nonEmbeddedRows = reranked.filter(r => !embeddedIdSet.has(r.id as number));
  1446	          const rerankedById = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>(
  1447	            reranked.map((result) => [canonicalResultId(result.id), result])
  1448	          );
  1449	
  1450	          const diversifiedRows = diversified.map((candidate): HybridSearchResult => {
  1451	            const existing = rerankedById.get(canonicalResultId(candidate.id));
  1452	            if (existing) {
  1453	              return existing;
  1454	            }
  1455	
  1456	            return {
  1457	              id: candidate.id,
  1458	              score: candidate.score,
  1459	              source: 'vector',
  1460	            };
  1461	          });
  1462	
  1463	          // Merge: diversified embedded rows first (MMR-ordered), then
  1464	          // non-embedded rows in their original relative order.
  1465	          reranked = [...diversifiedRows, ...nonEmbeddedRows];
  1466	        }
  1467	      } catch (embErr: unknown) {
  1468	        const msg = embErr instanceof Error ? embErr.message : String(embErr);
  1469	        console.warn(`[hybrid-search] MMR embedding retrieval failed: ${msg}`);
  1470	      }
  1471	    }
  1472	  }
  1473	
  1474	  // C138: Co-activation spreading — enrich with temporal neighbors
  1475	  const topIds = reranked
  1476	    .slice(0, SPREAD_ACTIVATION_TOP_N)
  1477	    .map(r => r.id)
  1478	    .filter((id): id is number => typeof id === 'number');
  1479	  if (topIds.length > 0) {
  1480	    try {
  1481	      const spreadResults: SpreadResult[] = spreadActivation(topIds);
  1482	      // Boost scores of results that appear in co-activation graph
  1483	      if (spreadResults.length > 0) {
  1484	        const spreadMap = new Map(spreadResults.map(sr => [sr.id, sr.activationScore]));
  1485	        for (const result of reranked) {
  1486	          const boost = spreadMap.get(result.id as number);
  1487	          if (boost !== undefined) {
  1488	            // M10 FIX: Update all score aliases so downstream consumers see the boost
  1489	            const boostedScore = ((result.score as number) ?? 0) + boost * CO_ACTIVATION_CONFIG.boostFactor;
  1490	            (result as Record<string, unknown>).score = boostedScore;
  1491	            if ('rrfScore' in result) (result as Record<string, unknown>).rrfScore = boostedScore;
  1492	            if ('intentAdjustedScore' in result) (result as Record<string, unknown>).intentAdjustedScore = boostedScore;
  1493	          }
  1494	        }
  1495	      }

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Vector Index Store
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Hybrid search pipeline
     5	// SEARCH: VECTOR INDEX
     6	// TypeScript port of the vector index implementation.
     7	// DECAY STRATEGY (ADR-004): Search-time temporal decay uses an
     8	// FSRS-preferred strategy. Memories with FSRS review data (last_review
     9	// IS NOT NULL, review_count > 0) use the FSRS v4 power-law formula:
    10	// R(t) = (1 + 0.2346 * t / S)^(-0.5)
    11	// Memories without review data fall back to half-life exponential:
    12	// Weight * 0.5^(days / half_life_days)
    13	// This ensures backward compatibility while aligning reviewed

    56	
    57	function loadSearchWeights(): SearchWeightsConfig {

    68	    }

    71	}

   116	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   117	   1. CONFIGURATION — Embedding Dimension
   118	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   119	
   120	/** Default embedding dimension used by the vector index. */
   121	export const EMBEDDING_DIM = 768;
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Gets the active embedding dimension for the current provider.
   125	 * @returns The embedding dimension.
   126	 */
   127	export function get_embedding_dim(): number {
   128	  return getStartupEmbeddingDimension();
   129	}
   130	
   131	/**
   132	 * Waits for the embedding provider to report a stable dimension.
   133	 * @param timeout_ms - The maximum time to wait in milliseconds.
   134	 * @returns A promise that resolves to the confirmed embedding dimension.
   135	 */
   136	export async function get_confirmed_embedding_dimension(timeout_ms = 5000): Promise<number> {
   137	  const start = Date.now();
   138	  while (Date.now() - start < timeout_ms) {
   139	    const dim = get_embedding_dim();
   140	    if (dim !== 768 || process.env.EMBEDDING_DIM === '768') {
   141	      return dim;
   142	    }
   143	    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
   144	  }
   145	  console.warn('[vector-index] Using default dimension 768 after timeout');
   146	  return 768;
   147	}
   148	
   149	type EmbeddingDimensionValidation = {
   150	  valid: boolean;
   151	  stored: number | null;
   152	  current: number | null;
   153	  reason?: string;
   154	  warning?: string;
   155	};
   156	
   157	type StoredEmbeddingDimension = {
   158	  existing_db: boolean;
   159	  stored_dim: number | null;
   160	  source: 'vec_metadata' | 'vec_memories' | null;
   161	  reason?: string;
   162	};
   163	
   164	function get_existing_embedding_dimension(
   165	  database: Database.Database,
   166	): StoredEmbeddingDimension {
   167	  const schema_row = database.prepare(`
   168	    SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_index'
   169	  `).get();
   170	
   171	  if (!schema_row) {
   172	    return {
   173	      existing_db: false,
   174	      stored_dim: null,
   175	      source: null,
   176	      reason: 'No existing schema',
   177	    };
   178	  }
   179	
   180	  const metadata_table = database.prepare(`
   181	    SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_metadata'
   182	  `).get();
   183	
   184	  if (metadata_table) {
   185	    const stored_row = database.prepare(`
   186	      SELECT value FROM vec_metadata WHERE key = 'embedding_dim'
   187	    `).get() as { value: string } | undefined;
   188	
   189	    if (stored_row) {
   190	      const stored_dim = parseInt(stored_row.value, 10);
   191	      if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
   192	        return {
   193	          existing_db: true,
   194	          stored_dim,
   195	          source: 'vec_metadata',
   196	        };
   197	      }
   198	    }
   199	  }
   200	
   201	  const vec_table = database.prepare(`
   202	    SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE name='vec_memories'
   203	  `).get() as { sql: string | null } | undefined;
   204	  const vec_sql = typeof vec_table?.sql === 'string' ? vec_table.sql : null;
   205	  const match = vec_sql?.match(/FLOAT\[(\d+)\]/i);
   206	
   207	  if (match) {
   208	    const stored_dim = parseInt(match[1], 10);
   209	    if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
   210	      return {
   211	        existing_db: true,
   212	        stored_dim,
   213	        source: 'vec_memories',
   214	      };
   215	    }
   216	  }
   217	
   218	  return {
   219	    existing_db: true,
   220	    stored_dim: null,
   221	    source: null,
   222	    reason: 'No stored vector dimension found for existing schema',
   223	  };
   224	}
   225	
   226	function validate_embedding_dimension_for_connection(
   227	  database: Database.Database | null,
   228	  _sqlite_vec_available: boolean,
   229	): EmbeddingDimensionValidation {
   230	  if (!database) {
   231	    return { valid: true, stored: null, current: null, reason: 'No database' };
   232	  }
   233	
   234	  try {
   235	    const current_dim = get_embedding_dim();
   236	    const existing = get_existing_embedding_dimension(database);
   237	
   238	    if (!existing.existing_db) {
   239	      return { valid: true, stored: null, current: current_dim, reason: existing.reason };
   240	    }
   241	
   242	    if (existing.stored_dim == null) {
   243	      return { valid: true, stored: null, current: current_dim, reason: existing.reason || 'No stored dimension' };
   244	    }
   245	
   246	    if (existing.stored_dim !== current_dim) {
   247	      const source_label = existing.source === 'vec_metadata' ? 'vec_metadata' : 'vec_memories schema';
   248	      const warning = `EMBEDDING DIMENSION MISMATCH: Existing database stores ${existing.stored_dim}-dim vectors (${source_label}), ` +
   249	        `but the active embedding configuration resolves to ${current_dim}. Refusing to bootstrap because vector search would fail. ` +
   250	        `Solutions: 1) Delete database and re-index, 2) Set EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER to match original, ` +
   251	        `3) Use MEMORY_DB_PATH for provider-specific databases.`;
   252	      console.error(`[vector-index] WARNING: ${warning}`);
   253	      return { valid: false, stored: existing.stored_dim, current: current_dim, warning };
   254	    }
   255	
   256	    return { valid: true, stored: existing.stored_dim, current: current_dim };
   257	  } catch (e: unknown) {
   258	    console.warn('[vector-index] Dimension validation error:', get_error_message(e));
   259	    return { valid: true, stored: null, current: get_embedding_dim(), reason: get_error_message(e) };
   260	  }
   261	}
   262	
   263	/**
   264	 * Validates that stored vector dimensions match the provider.
   265	 * @returns The validation result.
   266	 */
   267	export function validate_embedding_dimension(): { valid: boolean; stored: number | null; current: number | null; reason?: string; warning?: string } {
   268	  return validate_embedding_dimension_for_connection(db, sqlite_vec_available_flag);
   269	}
   270	
   271	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   272	   2. DATABASE PATH AND SECURITY
   273	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   274	
   275	// F4.04/F8.02 fix: Use centralized DB path from core/config.ts.
   276	// Legacy env vars MEMORY_DB_DIR and MEMORY_DB_PATH remain supported for backward compatibility.
   277	const DEFAULT_DB_DIR = process.env.MEMORY_DB_DIR
   278	  ? path.resolve(process.env.MEMORY_DB_DIR)
   279	  : path.dirname(DATABASE_PATH);
   280	/** Default path for the vector-index database file. */
   281	export const DEFAULT_DB_PATH = process.env.MEMORY_DB_PATH
   282	  || path.join(DEFAULT_DB_DIR, path.basename(DATABASE_PATH));
   283	const DB_PERMISSIONS = 0o600;
   284	
   285	function resolve_database_path() {

   287	}
   288

   290	const ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS = [

   292	  path.join(process.cwd(), '.opencode'),
   293	  path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude'),
   294	  process.cwd(),
   295	  ...(process.env.MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS ? process.env.MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS.split(path.delimiter) : [])
   296	].filter(Boolean).map(p => path.resolve(p));
   297	
   298	/**
   299	 * Validates a file path against allowed local base paths.
   300	 * @param file_path - The file path to validate.
   301	 * @returns The validated file path, if allowed.
   302	 */
   303	export function validate_file_path_local(file_path: unknown): string | null {
   304	  if (typeof file_path !== 'string') {
   305	    return null;
   306	  }
   307	
   308	  return validateFilePath(file_path, ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS);
   309	}

codex
The live DB inspection already surfaced one useful storage signal: the database depends on `sqlite-vec` strongly enough that the plain `sqlite3` CLI can’t even query `vec_memories` without the extension loaded. I’m drilling into schema creation now to pin down whether our design is “one canonical row store plus companion indexes” or something closer to Mnemosyne’s documented store shape.

memory_history|CREATE TABLE memory_history (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      prev_value TEXT,
      new_value TEXT,
      event TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(event IN ('ADD', 'UPDATE', 'DELETE')),
      timestamp DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
      is_deleted INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      actor TEXT DEFAULT 'system'
    , spec_folder TEXT)
checkpoints|CREATE TABLE checkpoints (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      spec_folder TEXT,
      git_branch TEXT,
      memory_snapshot BLOB,
      file_snapshot BLOB,
      metadata TEXT
    )
memory_entities|CREATE TABLE memory_entities (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            entity_text TEXT NOT NULL,
            entity_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'noun_phrase',
            frequency INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
            created_by TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'entity_extractor',
            created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
            UNIQUE(memory_id, entity_text)
          )
entity_catalog|CREATE TABLE entity_catalog (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            canonical_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
            aliases TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
            entity_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'noun_phrase',
            memory_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
            created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
          )
archival_stats|CREATE TABLE archival_stats (
        key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
        value TEXT NOT NULL,
        updated_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
      )
feedback_events|CREATE TABLE feedback_events (
    id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    type       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN (
                 'search_shown','result_cited','query_reformulated',
                 'same_topic_requery','follow_on_tool_use'
               )),
    memory_id  TEXT NOT NULL,
    query_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
    confidence TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(confidence IN ('strong','medium','weak')),
    timestamp  INTEGER NOT NULL,
    session_id TEXT
  )
session_state|CREATE TABLE session_state (
    session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK(status IN ('active', 'completed', 'interrupted')),
    spec_folder TEXT,
    current_task TEXT,
    last_action TEXT,
    context_summary TEXT,
    pending_work TEXT,
    state_data TEXT,
    created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
  , tenant_id TEXT, user_id TEXT, agent_id TEXT)
consumption_log|CREATE TABLE consumption_log (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
      query_text TEXT,
      intent TEXT,
      mode TEXT,
      result_count INTEGER,
      result_ids TEXT,
      session_id TEXT,
      timestamp TEXT,
      latency_ms REAL,
      spec_folder_filter TEXT,
      metadata TEXT
    )
