 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: "MCP Server Database Storage"
     3	description: "Runtime SQLite storage for memory, structural code graph, vectors, FTS, and coordination files."
     4	trigger_phrases:
     5	  - "database"
     6	  - "sqlite"
     7	  - "vector embeddings"
     8	---
     9	
    10	
    11	# MCP Server Database Storage
    12	
    13	<!-- ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->
    14	## TABLE OF CONTENTS
    15	
    16	- [1. OVERVIEW](#1--overview)
    17	- [2. IMPLEMENTED STATE](#2--implemented-state)
    18	- [3. HARDENING NOTES](#3--hardening-notes)
    19	- [4. OPERATIONAL NOTES](#4--operational-notes)
    20	- [5. RELATED](#5--related)
    21	
    22	<!-- /ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->
    23	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    24	## 1. OVERVIEW
    25	
    26	This section provides an overview of the MCP Server Database Storage directory.
    27	
    28	`database/` is the canonical runtime persistence location for the default MCP server configuration in this repository.
    29	
    30	The MCP server resolves its default database directory through `shared/paths.ts` and `core/config.ts`, which point to `mcp_server/database/` unless `MEMORY_DB_PATH`, `SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR`, or `SPECKIT_DB_DIR` override it at runtime.
    31	
    32	- Tracked in repo (source view): `.db-updated`, `.gitkeep`, `README.md`.
    33	- Runtime-generated (canonical): `database/context-index.sqlite`, `database/code-graph.sqlite`, and their WAL sidecars (`-wal`, `-shm`).
    34	- Embedding-profile database: `context-index__voyage__voyage-4__1024.sqlite` (and `-wal`, `-shm` sidecars) — profile-specific vector store.
    35	- Evaluation database: `speckit-eval.db` (and `-wal`, `-shm` sidecars) — stores ablation/baseline evaluation results.
    36	
    37	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    38	<!-- ANCHOR:implemented-state -->
    39	## 2. IMPLEMENTED STATE
    40	
    41	
    42	- Primary table set includes `memory_index`, `vec_memories`, `memory_fts`, `checkpoints`, and `config`.
    43	- Structural code-graph persistence lives in the separate `code-graph.sqlite` database with `code_files`, `code_nodes`, `code_edges`, and `code_graph_metadata`.
    44	- Document-type indexing alignment:
    45	  - `memory_index.document_type` and `memory_index.spec_level` are part of schema v13.
    46	  - Spec document indexing is first-class (not memory-only).
    47	  - Anchor-based retrieval applies to indexed spec docs.
    48	- `.db-updated` is used by `core/db-state.ts` to detect external writes and reinitialize connections.
    49	- Code-graph metadata stores freshness-relevant values such as `last_git_head`.
    50	
    51	
    52	<!-- /ANCHOR:implemented-state -->
    53	<!-- ANCHOR:hardening-notes -->
    54	## 3. HARDENING NOTES
    55	
    56	
    57	- Reinit safety is handled in `core/db-state.ts` (mutex + dependency rebind).
    58	- Rate-limit timestamp is persisted in DB `config` table (`last_index_scan`).
    59	- Document metadata remains preserved across update/reinforce/index paths (document-type indexing hardening).
    60	
    61	
    62	<!-- /ANCHOR:hardening-notes -->
    63	<!-- ANCHOR:operational-notes -->
    64	## 4. OPERATIONAL NOTES
    65	
    66	
    67	- Database files are intentionally not committed; only control files are tracked.
    68	- Primary memory index: `database/context-index.sqlite`.
    69	- Structural code-graph index: `database/code-graph.sqlite`.
    70	- Embedding-profile database: `context-index__voyage__voyage-4__1024.sqlite` stores profile-specific vectors (naming convention: `context-index__<provider>__<model>__<dims>.sqlite`).
    71	- Evaluation database: `speckit-eval.db` stores ablation and baseline evaluation results from `scripts/evals/`.
    72	- Use MCP tools (`memory_stats`, `memory_health`, `memory_index_scan`) for normal operations.
    73	- Structural reads (`code_graph_query`, `code_graph_context`) can perform bounded inline selective refresh against `code-graph.sqlite` when the stale set is small enough.
    74	- Empty or broadly stale structural states still require explicit `code_graph_scan` to rebuild the graph database.
    75	- For reindex operations, see the canonical runbook at [`scripts/memory/README.md`](../../scripts/memory/README.md).
    76	
    77	
    78	<!-- /ANCHOR:operational-notes -->
    79	<!-- ANCHOR:related -->
    80	## 5. RELATED
    81	
    82	
    83	- `../core/README.md`
    84	- `../handlers/README.md`
    85	- `../../references/memory/memory_system.md`
    86	<!-- /ANCHOR:related -->

   547	// R1: Shared early-return helper for lineage functions.
   548	function getExistingLineageTransition(
   549	  database: Database.Database,
   550	  memoryId: number,
   551	): RecordedLineageTransition | null {
   552	  const existing = getLineageRow(database, memoryId);
   553	  if (existing) {
   554	    return {
   555	      logicalKey: existing.logical_key,
   556	      versionNumber: existing.version_number,
   557	      rootMemoryId: existing.root_memory_id,
   558	      activeMemoryId: existing.memory_id,
   559	      predecessorMemoryId: existing.predecessor_memory_id,
   560	      transitionEvent: existing.transition_event,
   561	    };
   562	  }
   563	  return null;
   564	}
   565	
   566	/**
   567	 * Validates transition input combinations before persisting.
   568	 * Rejects invalid event/predecessor pairings and backwards timestamps.
   569	 */
   570	function validateTransitionInput(
   571	  transitionEvent: string,
   572	  predecessorMemoryId: number | null,
   573	  validFrom: string,
   574	  predecessor: LineageRow | null,
   575	): void {
   576	  if (transitionEvent === 'CREATE' && predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   577	    throw new Error(`E_LINEAGE: CREATE transition must not specify a predecessor (got ${predecessorMemoryId})`);
   578	  }
   579	  if (transitionEvent === 'SUPERSEDE' && predecessorMemoryId == null) {
   580	    throw new Error('E_LINEAGE: SUPERSEDE transition requires a predecessor');
   581	  }
   582	  // M6 FIX: Compare timestamps as numeric epoch values, not raw strings,
   583	  // to handle non-ISO strings and timezone-offset variants correctly.
   584	  if (predecessor && new Date(validFrom).getTime() < new Date(predecessor.valid_from).getTime()) {
   585	    throw new Error(
   586	      `E_LINEAGE: valid_from (${validFrom}) is earlier than predecessor valid_from (${predecessor.valid_from})`,
   587	    );
   588	  }
   589	}
   590	
   591	/**
   592	 * Seed lineage state from an existing memory row when no lineage entry exists yet.
   593	 *
   594	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
   595	 * @param memoryId - Memory version to seed into lineage tables.
   596	 * @param options - Optional actor, timestamps, and transition metadata.
   597	 * @returns Seeded lineage state for the requested memory version.
   598	 */
   599	export function seedLineageFromCurrentState(
   600	  database: Database.Database,
   601	  memoryId: number,
   602	  options: RecordLineageTransitionOptions = {},
   603	): RecordedLineageTransition {
   604	  bindHistory(database);
   605	  ensureLineageTables(database);
   606	
   607	  const cached = getExistingLineageTransition(database, memoryId);
   608	  if (cached) return cached;
   609	
   610	  const row = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
   611	  const logicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
   612	  const actor = options.actor ?? 'system';
   613	  const historyEvents = options.historyEvents ?? getSafeHistoryEvents(database, memoryId);
   614	  const validFrom = options.validFrom
   615	    ?? historyEvents[0]?.timestamp
   616	    ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.created_at ?? row.updated_at);
   617	
   618	  const seedTx = database.transaction(() => {
   619	    database.prepare(`
   620	      INSERT INTO memory_lineage (
   621	        memory_id,
   622	        logical_key,
   623	        version_number,
   624	        root_memory_id,
   625	        predecessor_memory_id,
   626	        superseded_by_memory_id,
   627	        valid_from,
   628	        valid_to,
   629	        transition_event,
   630	        actor,
   631	        metadata
   632	      ) VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, NULL, NULL, ?, NULL, ?, ?, ?)
   633	    `).run(
   634	      memoryId,
   635	      logicalKey,
   636	      memoryId,
   637	      validFrom,
   638	      options.transitionEvent ?? 'BACKFILL',
   639	      actor,
   640	      buildMetadata(row, actor, historyEvents),
   641	    );
   642	
   643	    upsertActiveProjection(database, logicalKey, memoryId, memoryId, normalizeTimestamp(row.updated_at ?? validFrom));
   644	  });
   645	
   646	  seedTx();
   647	
   648	  return {
   649	    logicalKey,
   650	    versionNumber: 1,
   651	    rootMemoryId: memoryId,
   652	    activeMemoryId: memoryId,
   653	    predecessorMemoryId: null,
   654	    transitionEvent: options.transitionEvent ?? 'BACKFILL',
   655	  };
   656	}
   657	
   658	/**
   659	 * Seed or append a lineage transition for a memory version.

   663	 * @param options - Transition details such as predecessor and actor.
   664	 * @returns Recorded lineage state for the requested memory version.
   665	 */
   666	export function recordLineageTransition(
   667	  database: Database.Database,
   668	  memoryId: number,
   669	  options: RecordLineageTransitionOptions = {},
   670	): RecordedLineageTransition {
   671	  bindHistory(database);
   672	  ensureLineageTables(database);
   673	
   674	  const cached = getExistingLineageTransition(database, memoryId);
   675	  if (cached) return cached;
   676	
   677	  for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= MAX_LINEAGE_VERSION_RETRIES; attempt += 1) {
   678	    // A1/B14: Wrap predecessor UPDATE + lineage INSERT + projection UPSERT in a transaction.
   679	    const recordTransitionTx = database.transaction(() => {
   680	      const row = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
   681	      const rowLogicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
   682	      const actor = options.actor ?? 'system';
   683	      const transitionEvent = options.transitionEvent ?? 'CREATE';
   684	      const historyEvents = options.historyEvents ?? getSafeHistoryEvents(database, memoryId);
   685	      const predecessorMemoryId = options.predecessorMemoryId ?? null;
   686	      const validFrom = options.validFrom ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.updated_at ?? row.created_at);
   687	
   688	      let logicalKey = rowLogicalKey;
   689	      let rootMemoryId = memoryId;
   690	      let versionNumber = 1;
   691	      let predecessor: LineageRow | null = null;
   692	
   693	      if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   694	        predecessor = getLineageRow(database, predecessorMemoryId);
   695	        if (predecessor) {
   696	          if (predecessor.logical_key !== rowLogicalKey) {
   697	            throw new Error(
   698	              `E_LINEAGE: predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} logical identity ${predecessor.logical_key} ` +
   699	              `does not match memory ${memoryId} logical identity ${rowLogicalKey}`,
   700	            );
   701	          }
   702	          logicalKey = predecessor.logical_key;
   703	          rootMemoryId = predecessor.root_memory_id;
   704	          versionNumber = predecessor.version_number + 1;
   705	        } else {
   706	          const seeded = seedLineageFromCurrentState(database, predecessorMemoryId, {
   707	            actor,
   708	            transitionEvent: 'BACKFILL',
   709	          });
   710	          if (seeded.logicalKey !== rowLogicalKey) {
   711	            throw new Error(
   712	              `E_LINEAGE: predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} logical identity ${seeded.logicalKey} ` +
   713	              `does not match memory ${memoryId} logical identity ${rowLogicalKey}`,
   714	            );
   715	          }
   716	          logicalKey = seeded.logicalKey;
   717	          rootMemoryId = seeded.rootMemoryId;
   718	          versionNumber = seeded.versionNumber + 1;
   719	          predecessor = getLineageRow(database, predecessorMemoryId);
   720	        }
   721	      }
   722	
   723	      if (attempt > 0) {
   724	        const latest = getLatestLineageRowForLogicalKey(database, logicalKey);

   804	
   805	/**
   806	 * Create a new append-only memory row and wire it into lineage state.
   807	 *
   808	 * @param params - Parsed memory payload and append-only lineage metadata.
   809	 * @returns Identifier of the newly inserted memory row.
   810	 */
   811	export function createAppendOnlyMemoryRecord(params: CreateAppendOnlyMemoryRecordParams): number {
   812	  bindHistory(params.database);
   813	  const appendTx = params.database.transaction(() => {
   814	    const memoryId = insertAppendOnlyMemoryIndexRow(params);
   815	    recordLineageTransition(params.database, memoryId, {
   816	      actor: params.actor ?? 'mcp:memory_save',
   817	      predecessorMemoryId: params.predecessorMemoryId,
   818	      transitionEvent: 'SUPERSEDE',
   819	    });
   820	    return memoryId;
   821	  });
   822	
   823	  return appendTx();
   824	}
   825	
   826	function resolveLogicalKey(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): string | null {
   827	  const row = getLineageRow(database, memoryId);
   828	  if (row) {
   829	    return row.logical_key;
   830	  }
   831	
   832	  const memoryRow = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
   833	  const projection = getActiveProjection(database, buildLogicalKey(memoryRow));
   834	  return projection?.logical_key ?? buildLogicalKey(memoryRow);
   835	}
   836	
   837	/**
   838	 * Return the full ordered lineage chain for the logical key behind a memory.
   839	 *
   840	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
   841	 * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
   842	 * @returns Ordered lineage snapshots from oldest to newest version.
   843	 */
   844	export function inspectLineageChain(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): ResolvedLineageSnapshot[] {
   845	  bindHistory(database);
   846	  ensureLineageTables(database);
   847	  const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
   848	  if (!lineage) {
   849	    return [];
   850	  }
   851	
   852	  return lineage.rows.map((row) => resolveSnapshotFromLineageRow(database, row));
   853	}
   854	
   855	/**
   856	 * Build a compact operator-facing summary for the lineage behind a memory.
   857	 *
   858	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
   859	 * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
   860	 * @returns Aggregated lineage summary when one exists.
   861	 */
   862	export function summarizeLineageInspection(
   863	  database: Database.Database,
   864	  memoryId: number,
   865	): LineageInspectionSummary | null {
   866	  bindHistory(database);
   867	  ensureLineageTables(database);
   868	  const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
   869	  if (!lineage) {
   870	    return null;
   871	  }
   872	  const { logicalKey, projection, rows } = lineage;
   873	
   874	  if (rows.length === 0) {
   875	    return null;
   876	  }
   877	
   878	  const transitionCounts: Record<LineageTransitionEvent, number> = {
   879	    CREATE: 0,
   880	    UPDATE: 0,
   881	    SUPERSEDE: 0,
   882	    BACKFILL: 0,
   883	  };
   884	  const actors = new Set<string>();
   885	  let hasVersionGaps = false;
   886	  let activeRows = 0;
   887	
   888	  for (let index = 0; index < rows.length; index += 1) {
   889	    const row = rows[index];
   890	    const expectedVersion = index + 1;
   891	    if (row.version_number !== expectedVersion) {
   892	      hasVersionGaps = true;
   893	    }
   894	    // B7: Use version_number ordering for predecessor chain validation
   895	    // rather than assuming sequential array positions match predecessor IDs.
   896	    if (index > 0) {
   897	      const prevRow = rows[index - 1];
   898	      if (prevRow && row.predecessor_memory_id !== prevRow.memory_id && row.version_number === prevRow.version_number + 1) {
   899	        hasVersionGaps = true;
   900	      }
   901	    }
   902	    if (row.valid_to == null) {
   903	      activeRows += 1;
   904	    }
   905	    transitionCounts[row.transition_event] += 1;
   906	    if (row.actor.trim().length > 0) {
   907	      actors.add(row.actor);
   908	    }
   909	  }
   910	
   911	  const activeVersion = projection
   912	    ? rows.find((row) => row.memory_id === projection.active_memory_id) ?? null
   913	    : rows.find((row) => row.valid_to == null) ?? null;
   914	
   915	  return {
   916	    logicalKey,
   917	    rootMemoryId: rows[0].root_memory_id,
   918	    activeMemoryId: projection?.active_memory_id ?? activeVersion?.memory_id ?? null,
   919	    activeVersionNumber: activeVersion?.version_number ?? null,
   920	    totalVersions: rows.length,
   921	    versionNumbers: rows.map((row) => row.version_number),
   922	    historicalMemoryIds: rows.slice(0, -1).map((row) => row.memory_id),
   923	    firstValidFrom: rows[0].valid_from,
   924	    latestValidFrom: rows[rows.length - 1].valid_from,
   925	    actors: [...actors],
   926	    transitionCounts,
   927	    hasVersionGaps,
   928	    hasMultipleActiveVersions: activeRows > 1,
   929	  };
   930	}

  1248	}
  1249	
  1250	/**
  1251	 * Compatibility wrapper used by roadmap tests and save flows for lineage writes.
  1252	 *
  1253	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
  1254	 * @param params - Memory identifier and transition metadata to record.
  1255	 * @returns Recorded lineage transition for the requested memory version.
  1256	 */
  1257	export function recordLineageVersion(
  1258	  database: Database.Database,
  1259	  params: {
  1260	    memoryId: number;
  1261	    actor?: string;
  1262	    predecessorMemoryId?: number | null;
  1263	    effectiveAt?: string;
  1264	    transitionEvent?: LineageTransitionEvent;
  1265	  },
  1266	): RecordedLineageTransition {
  1267	  if (typeof (database as Database.Database & { exec?: unknown }).exec !== 'function') {
  1268	    // B6: Mock path returns memoryId as root — predecessor's root is unavailable
  1269	    // without DB access, and using predecessor ID itself is incorrect.
  1270	    return {
  1271	      logicalKey: `mock:${params.memoryId}`,
  1272	      versionNumber: 1,
  1273	      rootMemoryId: params.memoryId,
  1274	      activeMemoryId: params.memoryId,
  1275	      predecessorMemoryId: params.predecessorMemoryId ?? null,
  1276	      transitionEvent: params.transitionEvent ?? 'CREATE',
  1277	    };
  1278	  }
  1279	
  1280	  return recordLineageTransition(database, params.memoryId, {
  1281	    actor: params.actor,
  1282	    predecessorMemoryId: params.predecessorMemoryId,
  1283	    transitionEvent: params.transitionEvent,
  1284	    validFrom: params.effectiveAt,
  1285	  });
  1286	}

  1288	/**
  1289	 * Compatibility wrapper that resolves the active snapshot for a memory target.

  1295	export function getActiveMemoryProjection(

  1298	): ResolvedLineageSnapshot | null {

  1301	
  1302	/**

  1309	export function resolveMemoryAsOf(
  1310	  database: Database.Database,

  1312	): ResolvedLineageSnapshot | null {

  1323	export function runLineageBackfill(

   822	    const retryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
   823	      ...overrides,
   824	      minSimilarity: SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
   825	    });

   844	
   845	// 11. HYBRID SEARCH
   846	
   847	/**
   848	 * Run multi-channel hybrid search combining vector, FTS, BM25, and graph results with per-source normalization.
   849	 * Prefer hybridSearchEnhanced() or searchWithFallback() instead. This function uses naive per-source
   850	 * min-max normalization which produces different orderings than the RRF pipeline in hybridSearchEnhanced().
   851	 * Retained as internal fallback only.
   852	 */
   853	async function hybridSearch(
   854	  query: string,
   855	  embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
   856	  options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
   857	): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
   858	  const {
   859	    limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT,
   860	    specFolder,
   861	    minSimilarity = 0,
   862	    useBm25 = isBm25Enabled(),
   863	    useFts = true,
   864	    useVector = true,
   865	    useGraph = true,
   866	    includeArchived = false,
   867	  } = options;
   868	
   869	  const results: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
   870	
   871	  // Vector search
   872	  if (useVector && embedding && vectorSearchFn) {
   873	    try {
   874	      const vectorResults = vectorSearchFn(embedding, {
   875	        limit,
   876	        specFolder,
   877	        minSimilarity,
   878	        includeConstitutional: false, // Handler manages constitutional separately
   879	        includeArchived,
   880	      });
   881	      for (const r of vectorResults) {
   882	        results.push({
   883	          ...r,
   884	          id: r.id as number,
   885	          score: (r.similarity as number) || 0,
   886	          source: 'vector',
   887	        });
   888	      }
   889	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   890	      const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   891	      console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Vector search failed: ${msg}`);
   892	    }
   893	  }
   894	
   895	  // FTS search
   896	  if (useFts) {
   897	    const ftsResults = ftsSearch(query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived });
   898	    results.push(...ftsResults);
   899	  }
   900	
   901	  // BM25 search
   902	  if (useBm25) {
   903	    const bm25Results = bm25Search(query, { limit, specFolder });
   904	    results.push(...bm25Results);
   905	  }
   906	
   907	  // Graph search
   908	  if (useGraph && graphSearchFn) {
   909	    try {
   910	      const graphResults = graphSearchFn(query, { limit, specFolder });
   911	      for (const r of graphResults) {
   912	        results.push({
   913	          ...r,
   914	          id: r.id as number | string,
   915	          score: (r.score as number) || 0,
   916	          source: 'graph',
   917	        });
   918	      }
   919	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   920	      const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   921	      console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Graph search failed: ${msg}`);
   922	    }
   923	  }
   924	
   925	  // Normalize scores per source before merging so one method's raw scale
   926	  // Does not dominate others during final ranking.
   927	  const bySource = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult[]>();
   928	  for (const r of results) {
   929	    const src = r.source || 'unknown';
   930	    if (!bySource.has(src)) bySource.set(src, []);
   931	    bySource.get(src)!.push(r); // non-null safe: has() guard above guarantees entry exists
   932	  }
   933	
   934	  const normalized: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
   935	  for (const [, group] of bySource) {
   936	    if (group.length === 0) continue;
   937	    const scores = group.map(r => r.score);
   938	    let min = Infinity;
   939	    let max = -Infinity;
   940	    for (const s of scores) {
   941	      if (s < min) min = s;
   942	      if (s > max) max = s;
   943	    }
   944	    const range = max - min;
   945	    for (const r of group) {
   946	      normalized.push({
   947	        ...r,
   948	        score: range > 0 ? (r.score - min) / range : (r.score > 0 ? 1.0 : 0),
   949	      });
   950	    }
   951	  }
   952	
   953	  // Deduplicate by ID (keep highest normalized score)
   954	  // LIMITATION (P1-1): When a result appears in multiple sources (e.g., vector + fts),
   955	  // Only the highest-scoring entry's `source` is preserved. Multi-source provenance
   956	  // Is lost here. To fix properly, HybridSearchResult would need a `sources: string[]`
   957	  // Field and downstream consumers would need to be updated accordingly.
   958	  const deduped = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
   959	  for (const r of normalized) {
   960	    const canonicalId = canonicalResultId(r.id);
   961	    const existing = deduped.get(canonicalId);
   962	    if (!existing || r.score > existing.score) {
   963	      deduped.set(canonicalId, r);
   964	    }
   965	  }
   966	
   967	  return Array.from(deduped.values())
   968	    .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
   969	    .slice(0, limit);
   970	}
   971	
   972	/**
   973	 * Enhanced hybrid search with RRF fusion.
   974	 * All search channels use synchronous better-sqlite3; sequential execution
   975	 * is correct — Promise.all would add overhead without achieving parallelism.
   976	 */
   977	async function hybridSearchEnhanced(
   978	  query: string,
   979	  embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
   980	  options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
   981	): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
   982	  const execution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, options);
   983	  if (execution) {
   984	    if (options.stopAfterFusion) {
   985	      return applyResultLimit(execution.fusedResults, options.limit);
   986	    }
   987	
   988	    return enrichFusedResults(query, execution, options);
   989	  }
   990	
   991	  return hybridSearch(query, embedding, options);
   992	}
   993	
   994	async function collectAndFuseHybridResults(
   995	  query: string,
   996	  embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
   997	  options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
   998	): Promise<HybridFusionExecution | null> {
   999	  try {
  1000	    const evaluationMode = options.evaluationMode === true;
  1001	    const lists: HybridFusionExecution['lists'] = [];
  1002	
  1003	    // Pipeline metadata collector (populated by flag-gated stages)
  1004	    const s3meta: Sprint3PipelineMeta = {};
  1005	
  1006	    // -- Stage A: Query Classification + Routing (SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER) --
  1007	    // When enabled, classifies query complexity and restricts channels to a
  1008	    // Subset (e.g., simple queries skip graph+degree). When disabled, all channels run.
  1009	    const routeResult = routeQuery(query, options.triggerPhrases);
  1010	    const allPossibleChannels: ChannelName[] = ['vector', 'fts', 'bm25', 'graph', 'degree'];
  1011	    const activeChannels = options.forceAllChannels
  1012	      ? new Set<ChannelName>(allPossibleChannels)
  1013	      : new Set<ChannelName>(routeResult.channels);
  1014	
  1015	    // Respect explicit caller channel disables across both the primary route and
  1016	    // every fallback tier. useGraph=false also disables the dependent degree lane.
  1017	    const allowedChannels = getAllowedChannels(options);
  1018	    for (const channel of allPossibleChannels) {
  1019	      if (!allowedChannels.has(channel)) activeChannels.delete(channel);
  1020	    }
  1021	
  1022	    const skippedChannels = allPossibleChannels.filter(ch => !activeChannels.has(ch));
  1023	
  1024	    if (skippedChannels.length > 0) {
  1025	      s3meta.routing = {
  1026	        tier: routeResult.tier,
  1027	        channels: routeResult.channels,
  1028	        skippedChannels,
  1029	        featureFlagEnabled: isComplexityRouterEnabled(),
  1030	        confidence: routeResult.classification.confidence,
  1031	        features: routeResult.classification.features as Record<string, unknown>,
  1032	      };
  1033	    }
  1034	
  1035	    // -- Stage E: Dynamic Token Budget (SPECKIT_DYNAMIC_TOKEN_BUDGET) --
  1036	    // Compute tier-aware budget early so it's available for downstream truncation.
  1037	    // When disabled, getDynamicTokenBudget returns the default 4000 budget with applied=false.
  1038	    const budgetResult = getDynamicTokenBudget(routeResult.tier);
  1039	    if (budgetResult.applied && !evaluationMode) {
  1040	      s3meta.tokenBudget = {
  1041	        tier: budgetResult.tier,
  1042	        budget: budgetResult.budget,
  1043	        applied: budgetResult.applied,
  1044	        featureFlagEnabled: isDynamicTokenBudgetEnabled(),
  1045	        configValues: DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET_CONFIG as unknown as Record<string, number>,
  1046	        // headerOverhead and adjustedBudget are patched in below after they are computed
  1047	        headerOverhead: 0,
  1048	        adjustedBudget: budgetResult.budget,
  1049	      };
  1050	    }
  1051	
  1052	    // Channel results collected independently, merged after all complete
  1053	    let semanticResults: Array<{ id: number | string; source: string; [key: string]: unknown }> = [];
  1054	    let ftsChannelResults: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
  1055	    let bm25ChannelResults: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
  1056	    const vectorEmbeddingCache = new Map<number, Float32Array>();
  1057	
  1058	    // All channels use synchronous better-sqlite3; sequential execution
  1059	    // Is correct — Promise.all adds overhead without parallelism.
  1060	
  1061	    // Vector channel — gated by query-complexity routing
  1062	    if (activeChannels.has('vector') && embedding && vectorSearchFn) {
  1063	      try {
  1064	        const vectorResults = vectorSearchFn(embedding, {
  1065	          limit: options.limit || DEFAULT_LIMIT,
  1066	          specFolder: options.specFolder,
  1067	          minSimilarity: options.minSimilarity || 0,
  1068	          includeConstitutional: false,
  1069	          includeArchived: options.includeArchived || false,
  1070	          includeEmbeddings: true,
  1071	        });
  1072	        semanticResults = vectorResults.map((r: Record<string, unknown>): { id: number | string; source: string; [key: string]: unknown } => ({
  1073	          ...r,
  1074	          id: r.id as number | string,
  1075	          source: 'vector',
  1076	        }));
  1077	        for (const result of semanticResults) {
  1078	          if (typeof result.id !== 'number') continue;
  1079	          const embeddingCandidate = toEmbeddingBufferView(
  1080	            (result as Record<string, unknown>).embedding
  1081	            ?? (result as Record<string, unknown>).embeddingBuffer
  1082	          );
  1083	          if (embeddingCandidate) {
  1084	            vectorEmbeddingCache.set(result.id, embeddingCandidate);
  1085	          }

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Vector Index Queries
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Hybrid search pipeline
     5	// Split from vector-index-store.ts — contains ALL query/search functions,
     6	// Content extraction, ranking, stats, cleanup, and integrity checks.
     7	
     8	import * as path from 'path';
     9	import * as fs from 'fs';
    10	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    11	import { formatAgeString as format_age_string } from '../utils/format-helpers.js';
    12	import { createLogger } from '../utils/logger.js';
    13	import { recordHistory } from '../storage/history.js';
    14	import * as embeddingsProvider from '../providers/embeddings.js';
    15	import {
    16	  get_error_message,
    17	  parse_trigger_phrases,
    18	  to_embedding_buffer,
    19	  VectorIndexError,
    20	  VectorIndexErrorCode,
    21	} from './vector-index-types.js';
    22	import {
    23	  initialize_db,
    24	  get_embedding_dim,
    25	  get_constitutional_memories,
    26	  init_prepared_statements,
    27	  validate_file_path_local,
    28	  safe_read_file_async,
    29	  safe_parse_json,
    30	  search_weights,
    31	  sqlite_vec_available as get_sqlite_vec_available,
    32	} from './vector-index-store.js';
    33	import { delete_memory_from_database } from './vector-index-mutations.js';
    34	import type {
    35	  EmbeddingInput,
    36	  EnrichedSearchResult,
    37	  MemoryRow,
    38	  VectorSearchOptions,
    39	} from './vector-index-types.js';
    40	
    41	const logger = createLogger('VectorIndex');
    42	
    43	type RelatedMemoryLink = { id: number; similarity: number };
    44	type UsageStatsOptions = { sortBy?: string; order?: string; limit?: number };
    45	type CleanupOptions = {
    46	  maxAgeDays?: number;
    47	  maxAccessCount?: number;
    48	  maxConfidence?: number;
    49	  limit?: number;
    50	};
    51	
    52	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    53	   SIMPLE QUERIES
    54	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    55	
    56	/**
    57	 * Gets one indexed memory by identifier.
    58	 * @param id - The memory identifier.
    59	 * @returns The matching memory row, if found.
    60	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates store initialization failures from the vector index.
    61	 * @example
    62	 * ```ts
    63	 * const memory = get_memory(42);
    64	 * ```
    65	 */
    66	export function get_memory(id: number, database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): MemoryRow | null {
    67	  const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
    68	  const row = stmts.get_by_id.get(id);
    69	
    70	  if (row) {
    71	    row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
    72	    row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
    73	  }
    74	
    75	  return row || null;
    76	}
    77	
    78	/**
    79	 * Gets indexed memories for a spec folder.
    80	 * @param spec_folder - The spec folder to query.
    81	 * @returns The memory rows for the folder.
    82	 */
    83	export function get_memories_by_folder(
    84	  spec_folder: string,
    85	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
    86	): MemoryRow[] {
    87	  const rows = database.prepare(`
    88	    SELECT m.*
    89	    FROM memory_index m
    90	    JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
    91	    WHERE m.spec_folder = ?
    92	    ORDER BY m.created_at DESC
    93	  `).all(spec_folder) as MemoryRow[];
    94	
    95	  return rows.map((row: MemoryRow) => {
    96	    row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
    97	    row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
    98	    return row;
    99	  });
   100	}
   101	
   102	/**
   103	 * Gets the total number of indexed memories.
   104	 * @returns The total memory count.
   105	 */
   106	export function get_memory_count(database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): number {
   107	  const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
   108	  const result = stmts.count_all.get();
   109	  return result?.count ?? 0;
   110	}
   111	
   112	/**
   113	 * Gets memory counts grouped by embedding status.
   114	 * @returns The counts for each embedding status.
   115	 */
   116	export function get_status_counts(
   117	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   118	): { pending: number; success: number; failed: number; retry: number; partial: number } {
   119	  const rows = database.prepare(`
   120	    SELECT m.embedding_status, COUNT(*) as count
   121	    FROM memory_index m
   122	    JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
   123	    GROUP BY m.embedding_status
   124	  `).all();
   125	
   126	  // M7 FIX: Include 'partial' status in counts
   127	  const counts = { pending: 0, success: 0, failed: 0, retry: 0, partial: 0 };
   128	  for (const row of rows as Array<{ embedding_status: keyof typeof counts; count: number }>) {
   129	    if (row.embedding_status in counts) {
   130	      counts[row.embedding_status] = row.count;
   131	    }
   132	  }
   133	
   134	  return counts;
   135	}
   136	
   137	/**
   138	 * Gets summary counts for indexed memories.
   139	 * @returns The aggregate memory statistics.
   140	 */
   141	export function get_stats(
   142	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   143	): { total: number; pending: number; success: number; failed: number; retry: number; partial: number } {
   144	  const counts = get_status_counts(database);
   145	  const total = counts.pending + counts.success + counts.failed + counts.retry + counts.partial;
   146	
   147	  return {
   148	    total,
   149	    ...counts
   150	  };
   151	}
   152	
   153	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   154	   VECTOR SEARCH
   155	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   156	
   157	/**
   158	 * Searches indexed memories by vector similarity.
   159	 * @param query_embedding - The query embedding to search with.
   160	 * @param options - Search options.
   161	 * @returns The matching memory rows.
   162	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates store initialization failures from the vector index.
   163	 * @example
   164	 * ```ts
   165	 * const rows = vector_search(queryEmbedding, { limit: 5, specFolder: 'specs/001-demo' });
   166	 * ```
   167	 */
   168	export function vector_search(
   169	  query_embedding: EmbeddingInput,
   170	  options: VectorSearchOptions = {},
   171	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   172	): MemoryRow[] {
   173	  const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
   174	  if (!sqlite_vec) {
   175	    console.warn('[vector-index] Vector search unavailable - sqlite-vec not loaded');
   176	    return [];
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  const {
   180	    limit = 10,
   181	    specFolder = null,
   182	    minSimilarity = 0,
   183	    useDecay = true,
   184	    tier = null,
   185	    contextType = null,
   186	    includeConstitutional = true,
   187	    includeArchived = false
   188	  } = options;
   189	
   190	  // M9 FIX: Validate embedding dimension before querying
   191	  const expected_dim = get_embedding_dim();
   192	  if (!query_embedding || query_embedding.length !== expected_dim) {
   193	    throw new VectorIndexError(
   194	      `Invalid embedding dimension: expected ${expected_dim}, got ${query_embedding?.length}`,
   195	      VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
   196	    );
   197	  }
   198	
   199	  const query_buffer = to_embedding_buffer(query_embedding);
   200	  const max_distance = 2 * (1 - minSimilarity / 100);
   201	
   202	  // ADR-004: FSRS-preferred decay with half-life fallback
   203	  const decay_expr = useDecay
   204	    ? `CASE
   205	         WHEN m.is_pinned = 1 THEN m.importance_weight
   206	         WHEN m.last_review IS NOT NULL AND m.review_count > 0 THEN
   207	           m.importance_weight * POWER(
   208	             1.0 + (19.0/81.0) * (julianday('now') - julianday(m.last_review)) / COALESCE(NULLIF(m.stability, 0), 1.0),
   209	             -0.5
   210	           )
   211	         ELSE m.importance_weight * POWER(0.5, (julianday('now') - julianday(m.updated_at)) / COALESCE(NULLIF(m.decay_half_life_days, 0), 90.0))
   212	       END`
   213	    : 'm.importance_weight';
   214	
   215	  let constitutional_results: MemoryRow[] = [];
   216	
   217	  if (includeConstitutional && tier !== 'constitutional') {
   218	    constitutional_results = get_constitutional_memories(database, specFolder, includeArchived);
   219	  }
   220	
   221	  const where_clauses = ['m.embedding_status = \'success\''];
   222	  const params: unknown[] = [query_buffer];
   223	
   224	  where_clauses.push('(m.expires_at IS NULL OR m.expires_at > datetime(\'now\'))');
   225	
   226	  if (!includeArchived) {
   227	    where_clauses.push('(m.is_archived IS NULL OR m.is_archived = 0)');
   228	  }
   229	  if (tier === 'deprecated') {
   230	    where_clauses.push('m.importance_tier = ?');
   231	    params.push('deprecated');
   232	  } else if (tier === 'constitutional') {
   233	    where_clauses.push('m.importance_tier = ?');
   234	    params.push('constitutional');
   235	  } else if (tier) {
   236	    where_clauses.push('m.importance_tier = ?');
   237	    params.push(tier);
   238	  } else {
   239	    where_clauses.push('(m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier NOT IN (\'deprecated\', \'constitutional\'))');
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  if (specFolder) {
   243	    where_clauses.push('m.spec_folder = ?');
   244	    params.push(specFolder);
   245	  }
   246	
   247	  if (contextType) {
   248	    where_clauses.push('m.context_type = ?');
   249	    params.push(contextType);
   250	  }
   251	
   252	  // M8 FIX: If constitutional results already satisfy limit, return them directly
   253	  if (constitutional_results.length >= limit) {
   254	    return constitutional_results.slice(0, limit);
   255	  }
   256	  const adjusted_limit = Math.max(1, limit - constitutional_results.length);
   257	  params.push(max_distance, adjusted_limit);
   258	
   259	  const sql = `
   260	    SELECT sub.*,
   261	           ROUND((1 - sub.distance / 2) * 100, 2) as similarity
   262	    FROM (
   263	      SELECT m.*, vec_distance_cosine(v.embedding, ?) as distance,
   264	             ${decay_expr} as effective_importance
   265	      FROM memory_index m
   266	      JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
   267	      JOIN vec_memories v ON m.id = v.rowid
   268	      WHERE ${where_clauses.join(' AND ')}
   269	    ) sub
   270	    WHERE sub.distance <= ?
   271	    ORDER BY (sub.distance - (sub.effective_importance * 0.1)) ASC
   272	    LIMIT ?
   273	  `;
   274	
   275	  const rows = database.prepare(sql).all(...params);
   276	
   277	  const regular_results = (rows as MemoryRow[]).map((row: MemoryRow) => {
   278	    row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
   279	    row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
   280	    return row;
   281	  });
   282	
   283	  return [...constitutional_results, ...regular_results];
   284	}
   285	
   286	/**
   287	 * Gets constitutional memories for prompt assembly.
   288	 * @param options - Retrieval options.
   289	 * @returns The constitutional memory rows.
   290	 */
   291	export function get_constitutional_memories_public(
   292	  options: { specFolder?: string | null; maxTokens?: number; includeArchived?: boolean } = {},
   293	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   294	): MemoryRow[] {
   295	  const { specFolder = null, maxTokens = 2000, includeArchived = false } = options;
   296	
   297	  let results = get_constitutional_memories(database, specFolder, includeArchived);
   298	
   299	  const TOKENS_PER_MEMORY = 100;
   300	  const max_count = Math.floor(maxTokens / TOKENS_PER_MEMORY);
   301	  if (results.length > max_count) {
   302	    results = results.slice(0, max_count);
   303	  }
   304	
   305	  return results;
   306	}
   307	
   308	/**
   309	 * Searches indexed memories with multiple concept embeddings.
   310	 * @param concept_embeddings - The concept embeddings to search with.
   311	 * @param options - Search options.
   312	 * @returns The matching memory rows.
   313	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} When concept count or embedding dimensions are invalid, or when store initialization fails.
   314	 * @example
   315	 * ```ts
   316	 * const rows = multi_concept_search([embA, embB], { limit: 8, specFolder: 'specs/001-demo' });
   317	 * ```
   318	 */
   319	export function multi_concept_search(
   320	  concept_embeddings: EmbeddingInput[],

codex
I’ve got enough of the storage picture to move from code collection into synthesis. The key distinction is emerging clearly: Mnemosyne’s plugin exposes a very small storage contract at the tool boundary, while Spec Kit Memory runs a much richer canonical row store with multiple derived indexes and state tables layered around it.

   491	/**
   492	 * Deletes a memory and its related index records.
   493	 * @param id - The memory identifier.
   494	 * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
   495	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates mutation or store initialization failures from the delete pipeline.
   496	 * @example
   497	 * ```ts
   498	 * const deleted = delete_memory(42);
   499	 * ```
   500	 */
   501	export function delete_memory(id: number, database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): boolean {
   502	  return delete_memory_from_database(database, id);
   503	}
   504	
   505	/**
   506	 * Deletes a memory and related index records using the provided database handle.
   507	 * @param database - The database containing the target memory.
   508	 * @param id - The memory identifier.
   509	 * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
   510	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates mutation failures encountered while deleting the memory.
   511	 * @example
   512	 * ```ts
   513	 * const deleted = delete_memory_from_database(database, 42);
   514	 * ```
   515	 */
   516	export function delete_memory_from_database(database: Database.Database, id: number): boolean {
   517	  const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
   518	
   519	  const delete_memory_tx = database.transaction(() => {
   520	    // Memory_history rows are intentionally preserved after deletion
   521	    // So DELETE audit events recorded by handlers persist as audit trail.
   522	
   523	    if (sqlite_vec) {
   524	      try {
   525	        database.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?').run(BigInt(id));
   526	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   527	        if (!isExpectedMissingVecMemoriesTable(e)) {
   528	          throw new VectorIndexError(
   529	            `Vector deletion failed for memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(e)}`,
   530	            VectorIndexErrorCode.MUTATION_FAILED,
   531	          );
   532	        }
   533	      }
   534	    }
   535	
   536	    // BUG-020: Clean ancillary records so deletes do not leave graph residue behind.
   537	    deleteAncillaryMemoryRows(database, id);
   538	
   539	    const result = database.prepare('DELETE FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').run(id);
   540	
   541	    clear_search_cache();
   542	    clear_constitutional_cache();
   543	
   544	    return result.changes > 0;
   545	  });
   546	
   547	  // BUG-021: Remove the BM25 document only after the source row is deleted.
   548	  const deleted = delete_memory_tx();
   549	  if (deleted) {
   550	    try {

   552	        bm25Index.getIndex().removeDocument(String(id));

   555	      // BEST-EFFORT BM25 CLEANUP MUST NOT MASK A SUCCESSFUL PRIMARY DELETE.
   556	    }
   557	  }
   558	  return deleted;
   559	}

  1099	- Use `codex features enable memories` and `codex features enable code_mode`, then start a new Desktop thread to test MCP surfacing; gate toggles persist in `~/.codex/config.toml` [Task 6]
  1100	- When startup background scan emits `contamination_audit` JSON on stdout, Codex MCP JSON-RPC can fail to handshake; redirect/silence that stream or delay MCP client init until scan completion [Task 7]

# Iteration 002: CORE DATA MODEL

## Focus
CORE DATA MODEL: Examine the storage layer - schemas, data structures, persistence, indexing, database design.

## Findings

### Finding 1: Mnemosyne’s storage layer is opaque at this repo boundary
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L38), [external/package.json:41](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L41), [external/README.md:82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: the plugin only shells out to `mnemosyne init/search/add/delete`; the published package ships only `dist`, and all FTS5/BM25/sqlite-vec/RRF details live in the README rather than in inspectable schema or migration code in this repo.
- **Why it matters for us**: this repo is not a storage-model blueprint. It proves a tool boundary and a backend contract, but not the backend’s actual table layout, migration strategy, or deletion/versioning semantics.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Affected subsystem**: storage architecture evaluation
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Mnemosyne’s scope model is collection-name based, while Public’s is row-scoped and path-stable
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:28](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L28), [external/README.md:55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L55), [lineage-state.ts:251](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L251), [lineage-state.ts:264](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L264), [vector-index-schema.ts:2322](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L2322)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne derives a project collection from the directory basename and reserves a separate global collection. Public instead stores scope on each row (`tenant_id`, `user_id`, `agent_id`, `session_id`, `shared_space_id`) and builds logical identity from spec folder, canonical path, anchor, and scoped hash when needed.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s model is ergonomically simple, but it is weaker as a canonical data key. Basename-derived collections are collision-prone across similarly named repos, while Public’s model is designed for durable identity and finer isolation.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Affected subsystem**: scoping and identity
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Public is built around one canonical row store plus derived search indexes
- **Source**: [vector-index-schema.ts:2299](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L2299), [vector-index-schema.ts:2365](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L2365), [vector-index-schema.ts:2384](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L2384), [vector-index-queries.ts:263](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-queries.ts#L263), [sqlite-fts.ts:197](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/sqlite-fts.ts#L197), [context-index.sqlite](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/context-index.sqlite)
- **Evidence type**: wrapper-confirmed for Public
- **What it does**: `memory_index` is the source of truth; `vec_memories` is a derived vector table; `memory_fts` is a derived FTS5 table kept in sync by triggers. The live DB currently shows `2480` `memory_index` rows, `2480` `memory_fts` rows, `1893` parent rows, and `587` chunk children.
- **Why it matters for us**: this is fundamentally different from Mnemosyne’s collection abstraction. Public is already a unified metadata store with multiple retrieval projections, so an Mnemosyne-style adoption would need to be a facade or adjunct, not a backend replacement.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Affected subsystem**: persistence and indexing
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Public preserves version history and active snapshots; Mnemosyne exposes only immediate CRUD at the plugin boundary
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [lineage-state.ts:811](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L811), [lineage-state.ts:1257](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L1257), [context-server.ts:1654](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1654), [vector-index-mutations.ts:516](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts#L516)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives the agent `delete(id)` and nothing storage-visible about lineage. Public has append-only version insertion, `memory_lineage`, `active_memory_projection`, startup backfill for missing projections, and delete cleanup that removes vector/index side effects rather than only the primary row.
- **Why it matters for us**: replacing Public’s storage model with a simpler delete-oriented contract would lose temporal resolution, active-version semantics, and integrity guarantees across companion indexes.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Affected subsystem**: lifecycle and mutation semantics
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public’s database is a full memory substrate, not just a recall index
- **Source**: [vector-index-schema.ts:1007](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L1007), [vector-index-schema.ts:1027](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L1027), [vector-index-schema.ts:1049](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L1049), [context-index.sqlite](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/context-index.sqlite), [database/README.md:42](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/README.md#L42)
- **Evidence type**: wrapper-confirmed for Public
- **What it does**: beyond retrieval, Public persists `working_memory`, `causal_edges`, `checkpoints`, `memory_summaries`, `memory_entities`, `entity_catalog`, `feedback_events`, `consumption_log`, and more. The live DB currently has `3246` causal edges, `1844` summaries, `69602` entity links, `16257` catalog entities, and only `176` rows with `document_type='memory'` versus `2304` indexed spec artifacts.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already indexing a broader knowledge graph and spec corpus than Mnemosyne’s documented “local document store” framing. That means the main transferable idea is ergonomic surfacing, not storage simplification.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Affected subsystem**: broader memory platform design
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 6: Extension-coupled vector storage raises operability requirements that Mnemosyne’s wrapper does not address
- **Source**: [README.md:83](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L83), [vector-index-store.ts:246](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L246), [vector-index-store.ts:248](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L248), [context-server.ts:1696](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1696), [database/README.md:34](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/README.md#L34)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne documents sqlite-vec and local embeddings, but the wrapper has no schema-health or migration surface. Public explicitly tracks schema version `25`, validates embedding dimensions, supports provider-specific DB variants, and rebuilds BM25 from DB state at startup. A plain `sqlite3` CLI inspection of `vec_memories` fails without `vec0`, which shows how extension-coupled this storage is operationally.
- **Why it matters for us**: if we ever prototype a standalone memory binary or plugin facade, it needs explicit health, schema, and inspection commands. Otherwise support and debugging get worse the moment vector storage diverges from the host runtime.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Affected subsystem**: operability and health tooling
- **Impact**: medium

## Sources Consulted
- [external/src/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts)
- [external/package.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json)
- [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md)
- [vector-index-schema.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts)
- [vector-index-store.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts)
- [vector-index-queries.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-queries.ts)
- [sqlite-fts.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/sqlite-fts.ts)
- [hybrid-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts)
- [lineage-state.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts)
- [vector-index-mutations.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts)
- [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts)
- [database/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/README.md)
- [context-index.sqlite](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/context-index.sqlite)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.86
- Questions addressed: schema visibility, scope keying, canonical row store vs collection store, vector and FTS persistence, lineage and deletion semantics, companion-table breadth, extension-coupled operability
- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo does not prove backend schema details; Public already runs a canonical multi-index SQLite design; basename-derived collection scoping is weaker than Public’s row-level identity model; Public’s lineage/projection tables are materially richer than Mnemosyne’s visible CRUD contract; any future binary/plugin facade needs explicit health and schema introspection

## Reflection
- What worked: reading the thin wrapper first, then inspecting `sqlite_master`, schema-creation code, and live table counts made the storage-model differences obvious very quickly
- What did not work: the external repo does not include the actual Mnemosyne backend, so storage internals remain README-level there; plain `sqlite3` inspection also breaks on `vec_memories` without the `sqlite-vec` extension loaded

## Recommended Next Focus
LIFECYCLE + MUTATION MODEL: trace document-ID flow, overwrite vs supersede semantics, delete hygiene, checkpoint/restore behavior, and what minimum health/introspection contract an optional external-binary memory facade would need before it is safe to prototype.

- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Mnemosyne’s scope model is collection-name based, while Public’s is row-scoped and path-stable
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:28](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L28), [external/README.md:55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L55), [lineage-state.ts:251](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L251), [lineage-state.ts:264](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L264), [vector-index-schema.ts:2322](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L2322)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne derives a project collection from the directory basename and reserves a separate global collection. Public instead stores scope on each row (`tenant_id`, `user_id`, `agent_id`, `session_id`, `shared_space_id`) and builds logical identity from spec folder, canonical path, anchor, and scoped hash when needed.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mnemosyne’s model is ergonomically simple, but it is weaker as a canonical data key. Basename-derived collections are collision-prone across similarly named repos, while Public’s model is designed for durable identity and finer isolation.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Affected subsystem**: scoping and identity
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Public is built around one canonical row store plus derived search indexes
- **Source**: [vector-index-schema.ts:2299](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L2299), [vector-index-schema.ts:2365](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L2365), [vector-index-schema.ts:2384](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L2384), [vector-index-queries.ts:263](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-queries.ts#L263), [sqlite-fts.ts:197](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/sqlite-fts.ts#L197), [context-index.sqlite](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/context-index.sqlite)
- **Evidence type**: wrapper-confirmed for Public
- **What it does**: `memory_index` is the source of truth; `vec_memories` is a derived vector table; `memory_fts` is a derived FTS5 table kept in sync by triggers. The live DB currently shows `2480` `memory_index` rows, `2480` `memory_fts` rows, `1893` parent rows, and `587` chunk children.
- **Why it matters for us**: this is fundamentally different from Mnemosyne’s collection abstraction. Public is already a unified metadata store with multiple retrieval projections, so an Mnemosyne-style adoption would need to be a facade or adjunct, not a backend replacement.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Affected subsystem**: persistence and indexing
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Public preserves version history and active snapshots; Mnemosyne exposes only immediate CRUD at the plugin boundary
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [lineage-state.ts:811](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L811), [lineage-state.ts:1257](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L1257), [context-server.ts:1654](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1654), [vector-index-mutations.ts:516](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts#L516)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives the agent `delete(id)` and nothing storage-visible about lineage. Public has append-only version insertion, `memory_lineage`, `active_memory_projection`, startup backfill for missing projections, and delete cleanup that removes vector/index side effects rather than only the primary row.
- **Why it matters for us**: replacing Public’s storage model with a simpler delete-oriented contract would lose temporal resolution, active-version semantics, and integrity guarantees across companion indexes.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Affected subsystem**: lifecycle and mutation semantics
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public’s database is a full memory substrate, not just a recall index
- **Source**: [vector-index-schema.ts:1007](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L1007), [vector-index-schema.ts:1027](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L1027), [vector-index-schema.ts:1049](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts#L1049), [context-index.sqlite](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/context-index.sqlite), [database/README.md:42](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/README.md#L42)
- **Evidence type**: wrapper-confirmed for Public
- **What it does**: beyond retrieval, Public persists `working_memory`, `causal_edges`, `checkpoints`, `memory_summaries`, `memory_entities`, `entity_catalog`, `feedback_events`, `consumption_log`, and more. The live DB currently has `3246` causal edges, `1844` summaries, `69602` entity links, `16257` catalog entities, and only `176` rows with `document_type='memory'` versus `2304` indexed spec artifacts.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already indexing a broader knowledge graph and spec corpus than Mnemosyne’s documented “local document store” framing. That means the main transferable idea is ergonomic surfacing, not storage simplification.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Affected subsystem**: broader memory platform design
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 6: Extension-coupled vector storage raises operability requirements that Mnemosyne’s wrapper does not address
- **Source**: [README.md:83](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L83), [vector-index-store.ts:246](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L246), [vector-index-store.ts:248](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L248), [context-server.ts:1696](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1696), [database/README.md:34](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/README.md#L34)
- **Evidence type**: both
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne documents sqlite-vec and local embeddings, but the wrapper has no schema-health or migration surface. Public explicitly tracks schema version `25`, validates embedding dimensions, supports provider-specific DB variants, and rebuilds BM25 from DB state at startup. A plain `sqlite3` CLI inspection of `vec_memories` fails without `vec0`, which shows how extension-coupled this storage is operationally.
- **Why it matters for us**: if we ever prototype a standalone memory binary or plugin facade, it needs explicit health, schema, and inspection commands. Otherwise support and debugging get worse the moment vector storage diverges from the host runtime.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Affected subsystem**: operability and health tooling
- **Impact**: medium

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.86
- Questions addressed: schema visibility, scope keying, canonical row store vs collection store, vector and FTS persistence, lineage and deletion semantics, companion-table breadth, extension-coupled operability
- Questions answered: Mnemosyne’s checked-out repo does not prove backend schema details; Public already runs a canonical multi-index SQLite design; basename-derived collection scoping is weaker than Public’s row-level identity model; Public’s lineage/projection tables are materially richer than Mnemosyne’s visible CRUD contract; any future binary/plugin facade needs explicit health and schema introspection

## Reflection
- What worked: reading the thin wrapper first, then inspecting `sqlite_master`, schema-creation code, and live table counts made the storage-model differences obvious very quickly
- What did not work: the external repo does not include the actual Mnemosyne backend, so storage internals remain README-level there; plain `sqlite3` inspection also breaks on `vec_memories` without the `sqlite-vec` extension loaded

## Recommended Next Focus
LIFECYCLE + MUTATION MODEL: trace document-ID flow, overwrite vs supersede semantics, delete hygiene, checkpoint/restore behavior, and what minimum health/introspection contract an optional external-binary memory facade would need before it is safe to prototype.
